Record overflow incidents in a scratch section at the bottom of this plan file while iterating (use as a working todo list, not a final deliverable). --- ## Phase 8 — Documentation sync By the time this phase runs, Phase 1 Step 3 has already moved the five files out of `docs/` and deleted the empty directory. Phase 8 is just the content rewrites that depend on Phase 3–6 outcomes. In-file updates: - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/full-spec.md` (formerly `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md`) — rewrite "Chrome" and "Layout patterns" sections to describe the new cohesive-chrome rule (global logo + Kyiv badge + randomised sonar on every slide except title). Drop stale "no chrome overlay on content slides" wording. - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/cohesive-chrome.md` (NEW) — write the Phase 3–5 rules: sonar-pool picker logic using the `bodySonarFor(slideIndex, slideId)` helper (slideId-hashed for stability under reorder, see Phase 2), badge placement tokens, BioSlide-as-body-slide rationale. - `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/translation-constraints.md` (formerly `docs/SLIDE_TRANSLATION.md`) — Phase 5 already trimmed the BioSlide-specific table. Nothing more to do here. - `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/calibration-methodology.md` (formerly `docs/TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION.md`) — unchanged in substance; confirm relative links work after the move. - `CLAUDE.md` — update the "Design System" Quick rules section: chrome now applies globally; font sizes live only in `theme.css`; randomised sonar picked per slide index deterministically. Replace `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` link with `.claude/skills/design-system/`. - Sanity gate: `rg "docs/(DESIGN_SYSTEM|TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION|SLIDE_TRANSLATION|slides-with-blurred-background|slides-with-side-images)"` returns **zero** lines repo-wide. Final `docs/` state: directory does not exist. All knowledge lives in `.claude/skills/{design-system,pixel-perfect-translation}/`. --- ## Critical files (full list, for reference during execution) **New:** - `.claude/skills/design-system/SKILL.md` - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/cohesive-chrome.md` - `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/SKILL.md` - `src/components/GlobalChrome.tsx` - `public/sonar/dou-sonar-01.png` … `-N.png` **Moved (via `git mv` from `docs/` into skill references folders — all five files):** - `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` → `.claude/skills/design-system/references/full-spec.md` - `docs/slides-with-blurred-background.md` → `.claude/skills/design-system/references/blurred-background-pattern.md` - `docs/slides-with-side-images.md` → `.claude/skills/design-system/references/side-images-pattern.md` - `docs/TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION.md` → `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/calibration-methodology.md` - `docs/SLIDE_TRANSLATION.md` → `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/translation-constraints.md` - `docs/` directory itself: deleted **Modified:** - `src/styles/theme.css` (central typography tokens — Phase 6a add, Phase 6b bump) - `src/styles/slides.css` (~40+ font-size rules → CSS vars; body-slide chrome classes; hero token swap; bio-hero deletion; `docs/TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION.md` comment refs updated at lines 739 + 928) - `src/components/Slide.tsx` (accept `chrome`, `slideIndex`, and `slideId` props; render appropriate chrome) - `src/components/SonarPattern.tsx` (body variant + pool picker using `bodySonarFor`; drop `'speaker'`) - `src/components/SlideChrome.tsx` (add Kyiv badge alongside existing logo) - `src/components/Presentation.tsx` (thread `slideIndex`/`chrome` into ``; no chrome rendered here) - `src/components/CodeBlock.tsx` (already uses `var(--font-size-code)` — verify no inline override remains) - `src/components/diagram/ProcessBox.tsx`, `ContainerBox.tsx`, `ConnectionLine.tsx`, `StageNode.tsx`, `AgentActor.tsx`, `HumanActor.tsx` (replace hardcoded SVG fontSize with var references) - `src/types/slides.ts` (add `chrome` field; drop `heroVariant`) - `src/slides/index.ts` (no content change — this is where the `slides` array lives; **not** `src/App.tsx`) - `src/slides/TitleSlide.tsx` (add `chrome: 'hero'`) - `src/slides/BioSlide.tsx` (rewrite as body slide; preserve 5-stage reveal map) - 5 slides with inline `--font-size-code` overrides (WarcraftFun, SkillsActivator, AutoApproveHook, AgentWorkflow, AgentTraces — MetaSkillsSlide has no override) - Any slide with inline SVG `fontSize=` (enumerate via `rg -n "fontSize" src/slides/` before starting Phase 6a) - `public/ASSET_PROVENANCE.md` (add rows for new sonar pool; extend licensing note) - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/full-spec.md` (formerly `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` — rewrite chrome + layout sections) - `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/translation-constraints.md` (formerly `docs/SLIDE_TRANSLATION.md` — trim BioSlide table) - `CLAUDE.md` (update Design System section; re-point doc links to `.claude/skills/…` paths) **Deleted (if unreferenced after rework):** - `public/dou-rings-speaker.png` - `.bio-hero*` CSS block in `src/styles/slides.css` --- ## Reusable existing utilities (do not reinvent) - **`computeScale` in `src/components/Slide.tsx`** — already implements 1920×1080 fixed-stage scaling. Title slide keeps using it. - **`src/components/SlideElements.tsx`** — `SectionHeader`, `SlideItem`, `Code`, `Emphasis`, `Quote`, `SlideLink`. All slide rewrites (BioSlide in particular) must compose these, not raw HTML. - **`CodeBlock` in `src/components/CodeBlock.tsx`** — the canonical code-panel. The 6 slides migrating off inline `--font-size-code` stay on this component. - **`SonarPattern` srcRect+stretch technique** (existing `title` variant) — reuse the same wrapper+inner-img pattern for every new body variant so calibration stays consistent. - **`SlideChrome` logo wrapper** — extend in place (add the Kyiv badge alongside the existing logo ``), do not fork. - **Existing `@fontsource/ibm-plex-sans`** import in `src/index.css` — already covers 400/600/700 latin + cyrillic. No new font work needed. - **`vite-plugin-imagemin`** already compresses PNGs at build time — just drop new sonar assets into `public/sonar/`, no extra step. --- ## Verification End-to-end acceptance criteria: 1. **Skills activate correctly.** In a fresh Claude Code session in this repo, mention "I need to add a slide" → the `design-system` skill should surface as a relevant skill. Mention "reproduce template slide 4 pixel-perfect" → the `pixel-perfect-translation` skill should surface. 2. **Font-size single-source-of-truth check.** Open `src/styles/theme.css`, change `--slide-text-body` from `2rem` to `3rem`. Every body slide's paragraph text resizes. Nothing else requires editing. Revert. 3. **Chrome parity check.** For every slide except `title`, a screenshot at 1920×1080 shows: - A sonar backdrop (varies between slides deterministically — navigating back to slide 3 shows the same sonar every time) - DOU logo top-right (unchanged from current title slide) - "Київ, 2026" top-left - The bottom panel (Timer, TerminalInput, SlideProgress) unchanged 4. **Title slide still pixel-perfect.** Compare screenshot of slide 1 against the pre-change version — zero visual diff. 5. **No overflow.** `bun run dev`, navigate every slide, visually confirm no text cropped. 6. **Build passes.** `bun run build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors. Bundle size does not regress by more than the size of the new sonar assets. 7. **Grep cleanliness.** `rg "fontSize" src/slides` returns **zero** results. `rg "--font-size-code:" src/slides` returns **zero** results. `rg "#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}" src/slides` returns only SVG fill/stroke constants. 8. **Doc parity.** `.claude/skills/design-system/references/full-spec.md` reflects the new cohesive-chrome rule. `CLAUDE.md` Quick rules match implementation. `rg "docs/(DESIGN_SYSTEM|TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION|SLIDE_TRANSLATION|slides-with-blurred-background|slides-with-side-images)"` returns **zero** results repo-wide. The `docs/` directory no longer exists. 9. **Skill content completeness.** Every invariant that previously lived in `docs/` is now reachable from one of the two SKILL.md files (either inline or via a link into its `reference/` folder). No knowledge is lost in the migration. Use the Chrome extension (`http://localhost:5173/dou-days-2026/`) for every visual verification step as mandated by `CLAUDE.md`. --- ## Phase ordering & cross-phase dependencies ``` 1a. Skills scaffold (SKILL.md + references/ with shims pointing at existing docs/) 1b. Validate skills activate in a fresh session 1c. Atomic docs/ → references/ move (git mv all 5 files; update every docs/ reference repo-wide; delete docs/) 2. Extract sonar pool from template PPTX → public/sonar/ 3. Generalize SonarPattern (body variant + picker), SlideChrome (Kyiv badge), add GlobalChrome wrapper 4. Extend Slide.tsx with chrome mode; thread slideIndex + slideId from Presentation; add chrome field to SlideDefinition; TitleSlide sets chrome:'hero' 5. Rework BioSlide (drop hero; preserve reveal map); delete .bio-hero CSS, speaker sonar asset, provenance row, SonarPattern speaker variant atomically; trim translation-constraints.md 6a. Add typography tokens; convert every font-size call site to var(); visual output unchanged 6b. Bump token values to aggressive sizes; hero tokens untouched 7. bun install + bun run build (typecheck gate); browser verification at 1920×1080 and 1440×900; split/trim any overflowing slide via src/slides/index.ts 8. Content rewrites in moved reference files; CLAUDE.md update; final sanity grep ``` Phases 1 and 2 are independent and can run in parallel. Phase 3 depends on Phase 2 (needs assets to reference). Phase 4 depends on Phase 3. Phase 5 depends on Phase 4. Phase 6a can start as early as Phase 1 (it touches theme.css/slides.css/slides, none of which Phase 1 moves). Phase 6b must wait for Phase 6a. Phase 7 is the gate; Phase 8 is cleanup.

toolUseResult.plan: # Converge the DOU Days 2026 deck onto a cohesive design system ## Context Today the deck is split in two halves: - **2 hero slides** (TitleSlide, BioSlide) — pixel-perfect 1920×1080 replicas of template slides 1 and 6, with DOU chrome (sonar rings, logo, "Київ, 2026" tag) baked inline. - **22 body slides** — clean terminal layouts with the DOU palette and IBM Plex Sans font, but with **no** DOU chrome at all (no sonar, no logo, no city badge). Typography already honours a 2-sizes rule (`--font-size-h2` + `--slide-text-normal`) but with multiple inline overrides in code blocks and SVGs. The goal is to stop treating the template as a one-off reference for two slides and start treating it as the design system for the entire deck. Every slide except the title should share: 1. A sonar backdrop (picked deterministically per slide from a pool of template-extracted variants) 2. A persistent top chrome: DOU logo top-right + "Київ, 2026" top-left 3. The existing bottom panel (Timer, TerminalInput, SlideProgress) — unchanged 4. One set of aggressively large fonts, controllable from a single file 5. The existing React layout/reveal logic — adjusted only where it can line up with template composition Two project-level skills are added to codify the design and calibration knowledge so future edits stay on spec. --- ## Phase 1 — Fully absorb `docs/` into two project-level skills The `docs/` folder becomes redundant. Every piece of design-system and calibration knowledge moves into the skills, where it is activatable by Claude Code and still editable as plain markdown. New directory layout, mirroring the existing `.claude/skills/generate-image/` pattern and using the `references/` (plural) sub-file convention used across the superhuman-aidev marketplace (meta, cicd, product-dev, codexcli, golden-path all use `references/`). SKILL.md stays short for trigger/priming; bulky content lives in `references/*.md` that SKILL.md links to and Claude loads on demand: ``` .claude/skills/ ├── design-system/ │ ├── SKILL.md │ └── references/ │ ├── full-spec.md ← absorbs docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md │ ├── blurred-background-pattern.md ← absorbs docs/slides-with-blurred-background.md │ ├── side-images-pattern.md ← absorbs docs/slides-with-side-images.md │ └── cohesive-chrome.md ← NEW: codifies Phases 3–5 rules (random sonar pool, Kyiv badge, body-slide layout) └── pixel-perfect-translation/ ├── SKILL.md └── references/ ├── calibration-methodology.md ← absorbs docs/TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION.md └── translation-constraints.md ← absorbs docs/SLIDE_TRANSLATION.md (after Phase 5 trim) ``` **Migration ordering (revised, safer):** Create skill files and SKILL.md *first*, pointing their `references/` links at the existing `docs/` paths. Validate skills activate correctly in a fresh session (Codex's "safer docs migration" suggestion). Then, as the final step of Phase 1, `git mv` the docs files into `references/` and update the SKILL.md links in one atomic commit. Nothing is deleted until the moved files are validated in place. ### `.claude/skills/design-system/SKILL.md` Frontmatter (natural-language triggers, minimal tool scope): ```yaml --- name: design-system description: Apply the DOU Days 2026 deck design system when editing slides, changing colors or typography, updating palette or font tokens, adjusting chrome (logo, Kyiv badge, sonar backdrop), or matching the DOU template look. Use whenever touching src/slides/, src/styles/, or any component that renders slide chrome. allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Edit --- ``` SKILL.md body (concise, ~120 lines max): - **Invariants at a glance** (palette tokens, IBM Plex Sans, centralised typography in `src/styles/theme.css`, 2–3 font-sizes rule) - **Chrome rules** (logo + Kyiv badge + randomised sonar backdrop on every slide except `title`) - **Forbidden** (inline `fontSize`/`style={{fontSize:…}}`, hardcoded hex in JSX, new CSS classes before checking existing ones, glow/scanline/phosphor) - **Checklist before shipping** (no inline fonts, no new hex, slides fit viewport, visual parity verified via Chrome extension on `bun run dev`) - **Deep reference** links to: - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/full-spec.md` — complete palette, typography, layout patterns, chrome, do/don'ts - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/blurred-background-pattern.md` — full-screen image + blurred content box pattern - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/side-images-pattern.md` — full-height side-image layout pattern - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/cohesive-chrome.md` — body-slide chrome composition rules, sonar pool picker logic, badge placement tokens ### `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/SKILL.md` Frontmatter (natural-language triggers, minimal tool scope): ```yaml --- name: pixel-perfect-translation description: Recreate a DOU Days 2026 template slide in React/CSS at pixel parity. Use when building or tuning a hero slide, extracting assets from the template deck, matching template positioning exactly, calibrating a fixed 1920×1080 stage, translating hero copy into Ukrainian without breaking layout, or working with EMU, srcRect, or stretch image cropping. allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Edit, Bash(unzip:*), Bash(ffmpeg:*), Bash(magick:*), Bash(identify:*) --- ``` SKILL.md body (concise, ~100 lines max): - **Methodology summary** (1920×1080 fixed stage + `transform: scale()` in `src/components/Slide.tsx`, EMU→px conversion at 4762.5 EMU/px, srcRect+stretch via wrapper-size + inner-img-offset) - **Extraction quickstart** (download template PPTX from Google Slides → `unzip` → locate `ppt/media/imageN.png` + `ppt/slides/slideN.xml` for srcRect/spPr coordinates → convert EMU to stage px → save to `public/sonar/` → append row to `public/ASSET_PROVENANCE.md`) - **Font-size uplift rule** (bump ~30–40% above template for back-row readability in 400+ seat halls) - **Tuning order for Ukrainian translations** (rephrase → tracking → font size → widen container (never past calibrated max) → accept wrap) - **Deep reference** links to: - `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/calibration-methodology.md` — full EMU math, worked example for title slide, srcRect+stretch recipe - `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/translation-constraints.md` — hero slide translation rules, glyph-count checklist (scoped to any future hero slides; BioSlide content removed per Phase 5) ### Migration mechanics (revised order) **Step 1 — Stand up skills without moving docs yet.** Create both SKILL.md files and the `references/` directories. For each reference file, create a thin shim that says "See [docs/ORIGINAL.md](…)" so SKILL.md already has working links pointing at the existing docs. This lets Phase 1 ship as an atomic skill-activation change without touching `docs/`. **Step 2 — Validate skills activate.** In a fresh Claude Code session in this repo, verify both skills appear relevant when the trigger conditions are stated in natural language (e.g., "I'm editing a slide's colors" → design-system; "I need to recreate a template slide exactly" → pixel-perfect-translation). **Step 3 — Atomic docs migration.** In a single commit: - `git mv docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md .claude/skills/design-system/references/full-spec.md` - `git mv docs/slides-with-blurred-background.md .claude/skills/design-system/references/blurred-background-pattern.md` - `git mv docs/slides-with-side-images.md .claude/skills/design-system/references/side-images-pattern.md` - `git mv docs/TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION.md .claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/calibration-methodology.md` - `git mv docs/SLIDE_TRANSLATION.md .claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/translation-constraints.md` - Replace every `docs/...` reference repo-wide (including `src/styles/slides.css:739`, `src/styles/slides.css:928`, `CLAUDE.md`, and any cross-links inside the moved files) with the new skill-references path. Run `rg "docs/" --type-not md` + `rg "docs/[A-Z]"` to catch all hits. - Replace the reference shims from Step 1 with the actual (just-moved) content link. - Remove the now-empty `docs/` directory. **Step 4 — Add net-new prose.** `.claude/skills/design-system/references/cohesive-chrome.md` is the only genuinely new doc; it captures the Phase 3–5 rules. Write it as part of Phase 3 when the implementation lands. --- ## Phase 2 — Expand the sonar asset pool Currently only `public/dou-sonar-hero.png` and `public/dou-rings-speaker.png` exist. Extract **6–8 additional sonar/ring backgrounds** from the DOU Days 2026 dark speaker template (https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/REDACTED): 1. Export the deck as PPTX → `unzip` → inspect `ppt/media/image*.png` for every sonar/ring-style image used on content slides. 2. For each, record the source slide, srcRect crop (from the sibling `ppt/slides/slideN.xml`), and final stage dimensions in EMU → px via the 4762.5 EMU/px rule. 3. Save as `public/sonar/dou-sonar-01.png` … `public/sonar/dou-sonar-N.png` (new subfolder). 4. Keep `public/dou-sonar-hero.png` (title-slide specific). `public/dou-rings-speaker.png` is retired in Phase 5. 5. Append rows to `public/ASSET_PROVENANCE.md` — one row per extracted asset (source slide, dimensions at capture, srcRect if relevant, role). **Preserve the existing "DOU Days 2026 speaker rights" licensing note** and extend it to cover each new asset so the same usage-scope warning applies to the expanded pool (Codex-flagged IP concern). ### Picker strategy (deterministic, stable under slide reordering) - Function: `bodySonarFor(slideIndex, slideId)` returns `pool[(slideIndex + hash(slideId)) % pool.length]` — mixing `slideId` into the index means reordering slides in `src/slides/index.ts` does **not** reshuffle every slide's sonar, only the moved one's. This is important if the user changes talk ordering late in rehearsal. - Alternative: pure `pool[slideIndex % pool.length]`. Simpler but reshuffles on every reorder. - **Pick the `slideId`-hashed variant.** Document the stability guarantee (adding / removing slides changes sonars for slides at and after the insertion point; reordering only changes the moved slide) in `.claude/skills/design-system/references/cohesive-chrome.md`. Files touched: - `public/sonar/*.png` (new) - `public/ASSET_PROVENANCE.md` (extend + preserve licensing note) --- ## Phase 3 — Generalize chrome components ### `src/components/SonarPattern.tsx` Extend the existing `variant` prop (`'title' | 'speaker'`) to a **pool + picker** model: - New export: `const BODY_SONAR_POOL = ['dou-sonar-01.png', 'dou-sonar-02.png', …]` (array of asset filenames under `public/sonar/`) - New props: `slideIndex?: number` and `slideId?: string`. When `variant === 'body'`, pick via the `bodySonarFor(slideIndex, slideId)` helper defined in Phase 2 (slideId-hashed for stability under reorder). - Preserve the existing `'title'` variant exactly (title slide is still pixel-perfect). - Retire the `'speaker'` variant in Phase 5 (BioSlide no longer needs it). - The body variant renders full-bleed behind slide content at reduced opacity (e.g. `opacity: 0.35` via CSS var `--sonar-body-opacity`) so text stays legible. ### `src/components/SlideChrome.tsx` Currently renders only the top-right DOU logo. Extend to also render the "Київ, 2026" badge top-left. **Single-mode component — no `variant` prop.** Both hero and global chrome show the same logo + Kyiv badge; the only thing that changes between hero and non-hero slides is the sonar asset, which lives in `SonarPattern`, not here. - New JSX element `Київ, 2026` inside the existing chrome wrapper. - New CSS class `.slide-chrome__city-badge` in `src/styles/slides.css`, positioned via CSS variables: - `--chrome-badge-top`, `--chrome-badge-left`, `--chrome-badge-size` - The existing logo `` stays untouched — keep the srcRect+stretch replication exactly (don't regress calibration). - Both logo and badge scale together because the chrome wrapper sits inside the 1920×1080 stage that `Slide.tsx` scales. This keeps `SlideChrome` and `GlobalChrome` composable without overlap: `SlideChrome` = logo + Kyiv badge; `SonarPattern` = backdrop; `GlobalChrome` = SonarPattern(body) + SlideChrome; hero chrome = SonarPattern(title) + SlideChrome. No `variant` prop needed anywhere. ### New wrapper `src/components/GlobalChrome.tsx` Composes `` + `` into a single element that `Slide.tsx` renders whenever `chrome === 'global'`. Accepts `slideIndex` and `slideId` and passes them to SonarPattern. No other props. Files touched: - `src/components/SonarPattern.tsx` - `src/components/SlideChrome.tsx` - `src/components/GlobalChrome.tsx` (new) - `src/styles/slides.css` --- ## Phase 4 — Wire chrome into Slide.tsx (not Presentation), opt title slide out **Chrome owner = `src/components/Slide.tsx`, not `src/components/Presentation.tsx`.** Today the hero chrome (logo, sonar) is composed inside the slide wrapper where it shares the stage/scaling/stacking model. Adding a second chrome injection point in `Presentation.tsx` would create z-index and animation hazards. Cleaner: extend `Slide.tsx` to accept a `chrome` mode plus `slideIndex` and `slideId` props and render the right chrome inline. ### `src/components/Slide.tsx` - Add `chrome?: 'hero' | 'global' | 'none'` prop (default = `'global'`). - Add `slideIndex: number` and `slideId: string` props. Both are required to reach `SonarPattern` so the `bodySonarFor(slideIndex, slideId)` picker from Phase 2 is actually implementable end-to-end. (Codex R2: "sonar-picker wiring is still incomplete" — resolved.) - Render `` + `` when `chrome === 'hero'` (existing behaviour; TitleSlide). The Kyiv badge added in Phase 3 belongs to `SlideChrome` unconditionally — no `variant` prop needed on `SlideChrome` (see Phase 3 note below). - Render `` when `chrome === 'global'` (every slide except title; after Phase 5 also BioSlide). - Render nothing when `chrome === 'none'` (escape hatch, unused today). - Keep `transform: scale(...)` math untouched — chrome sits inside the scaled stage so `slideIndex`-based sonar picks scale identically to hero sonar. ### `src/components/Presentation.tsx` - Pass `slideIndex={currentIndex}`, `slideId={currentSlide.id}`, and `chrome={currentSlide.chrome ?? 'global'}` into ``. - The bottom panel (Timer, TerminalInput, SlideProgress) is **not** touched. No changes to its markup or layout. ### `src/types/slides.ts` - Add `chrome?: 'hero' | 'global' | 'none'` to `SlideDefinition`. - Retire the `heroVariant` field (`'title' | 'speaker'`) — after Phase 5, only TitleSlide is a hero and its variant is implicit. ### `src/slides/index.ts` and `src/slides/TitleSlide.tsx` - **Slide registration lives in `src/slides/index.ts`** (single exported `slides` array), **not** `src/App.tsx`. `src/App.tsx` only renders ``. - `TitleSlide` sets `chrome: 'hero'` in its `SlideDefinition`. - `BioSlide` drops `hero: true` and `heroVariant: 'speaker'` in Phase 5 — defaults to `chrome: 'global'`. - Every other slide inherits the default and needs no per-slide change. Files touched: - `src/components/Slide.tsx` - `src/components/Presentation.tsx` (only to thread `slideIndex`/`chrome` props) - `src/types/slides.ts` - `src/slides/TitleSlide.tsx` (add `chrome: 'hero'`) --- ## Phase 5 — Rework BioSlide into a body slide BioSlide is currently a 1920×1080 hero replica of template slide 6. User wants it converged to the body-slide pattern so it picks up the same global chrome as the rest of the deck. **Sequencing note:** this phase runs *after* Phase 1's atomic docs move, so the translation-constraints trim targets the new path at `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/translation-constraints.md`, not `docs/SLIDE_TRANSLATION.md`. Steps: 1. **Read current BioSlide reveal map.** BioSlide declares `maxRevealStages: 5` with the first item visible at stage 0 (`src/slides/BioSlide.tsx:38`). Record the exact stage-to-content mapping before rewriting so the new version keeps identical `revealStage >= N` guards. Any reorder is a behavioural change, not a refactor — call it out in the commit. 2. **Rewrite `src/slides/BioSlide.tsx`** as a Two-Column Text+Visual body slide: - Remove hero wrapper (`.bio-hero*`) and fixed pixel coordinates. - Drop `hero: true` and `heroVariant: 'speaker'` from the `SlideDefinition`. It now defaults to `chrome: 'global'` per Phase 4. - Left column: `` ("Хто я?"), current Ukrainian bio copy using `` for bullet reveals, guarded with the exact same `revealStage >= N` values from the pre-rewrite reveal map. - Right column: speaker photo as a plain `` if that asset exists; otherwise keep the text-only variant currently used in bio reveals 1–4. - Typography via the centralised CSS variables from Phase 6 — no inline fontSize. 3. **Clean up assets and CSS atomically:** - Remove every `.bio-hero*` rule from `src/styles/slides.css` in the same commit as the BioSlide rewrite (after `rg bio-hero src/` confirms no remaining references). - Remove the `'speaker'` branch from `src/components/SonarPattern.tsx`. - Delete `public/dou-rings-speaker.png` **and** remove its provenance row in `public/ASSET_PROVENANCE.md` in the same commit. Both must land together so provenance never references a missing asset. 4. **Trim translation constraints** at `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/translation-constraints.md` (post-Phase-1 path): strip out the BioSlide-specific container widths / glyph-count tables (they describe a hero layout that no longer exists). Keep the general hero-slide translation methodology for any future hero work. Files touched: - `src/slides/BioSlide.tsx` - `src/styles/slides.css` (remove `.bio-hero*` block) - `src/components/SonarPattern.tsx` (remove `'speaker'` variant) - `public/dou-rings-speaker.png` (delete) - `public/ASSET_PROVENANCE.md` (remove speaker-rings row) - `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/translation-constraints.md` (trim BioSlide section) --- ## Phase 6 — Centralised typography (split into 6a mechanical cleanup + 6b aggressive size bump) **Single source of truth:** `src/styles/theme.css`. Every font size in the deck reads from a CSS variable defined there. The scope is far broader than the original plan admitted — see the full inventory below. **Rollout split (per Codex's suggestion — isolates mechanical cleanup from visual retuning):** - **Phase 6a**: introduce all new tokens and convert every existing font-size declaration to reference a token. Visual output is **identical** to today. Any regression here is a pure bug, not a design change. - **Phase 6b**: bump the token values to the aggressive target sizes. Visual output changes. Overflow fixes handled in Phase 7. ### Full font-size inventory to migrate **Inline `fontSize` in TSX** — found via `rg "fontSize" src/`: - `src/components/CodeBlock.tsx` lines 10, 23 — already uses `var(--font-size-code)`, good. - `src/components/diagram/StageNode.tsx:66` — `fontSize={fontSize}` (prop-driven, already indirect). - `src/components/diagram/ProcessBox.tsx:46,60` — hardcoded `"13"`, `"10"`. - `src/components/diagram/ContainerBox.tsx:52` — hardcoded `"10"`. - `src/components/diagram/AgentActor.tsx:50` — computed `size * 0.22`. - `src/components/diagram/HumanActor.tsx:50` — computed `size * 0.2`. - `src/components/diagram/ConnectionLine.tsx:65` — hardcoded `"11"`. - Any remaining inline `fontSize=` in slide SVGs (e.g. `StickerFunSlide.tsx`, `ClaudeCodeSlide.tsx`, `EveryOrgRightNowSlide.tsx`, `InceptionPatternsSlide.tsx`, `AIInnovatorCurveSlide.tsx`) — enumerate with `rg -n "fontSize" src/slides/`. **Inline `style={{ '--font-size-code': '…' }}` overrides** — verified 5 slides (not 6; MetaSkillsSlide has none): - `src/slides/WarcraftFunSlide.tsx` - `src/slides/SkillsActivatorSlide.tsx` - `src/slides/AutoApproveHookSlide.tsx` - `src/slides/AgentWorkflowSlide.tsx` - `src/slides/AgentTracesSlide.tsx` **Font-size rules in `src/styles/slides.css`** — roughly 40+ declarations (verified via `rg -n "font-size" src/styles/slides.css`) covering hero blocks, body layouts, code panels, diagrams, and media-query breakpoints. Every single one becomes a `var(--…)` reference in Phase 6a. ### New tokens in `src/styles/theme.css` (Phase 6a) ```css :root { /* --- canonical slide typography (tokens only; values stay conservative in 6a) --- */ --slide-text-h2: 3.5rem; /* Phase 6b bumps to 4.5rem */ --slide-text-body: 1.35rem; /* Phase 6b bumps to 2rem */ --slide-text-code: 0.85rem; /* Phase 6b bumps to 1.25rem */ --slide-text-caption: 0.9rem; /* small labels, diagram annotations */ --slide-text-svg-sm: 10px; --slide-text-svg-md: 13px; --slide-text-svg-lg: 17px; /* --- title-slide hero calibration (do NOT change these in 6b — title slide parity) --- */ --hero-title-size: 94px; --hero-tagline-size: 56px; --hero-speaker-size: 38px; --hero-badge-size: 34px; --hero-description-size: 32px; /* --- chrome badge sizes (body slides) --- */ --chrome-badge-size: 28px; --chrome-logo-width: 364px; /* aliases so existing references keep working mid-migration */ --font-size-h2: var(--slide-text-h2); --slide-text-normal: var(--slide-text-body); --font-size-code: var(--slide-text-code); } ``` ### Phase 6a migration steps (mechanical) 1. Add every token above to `src/styles/theme.css`. 2. For every hit of `font-size:` in `src/styles/slides.css`, replace the literal with the closest-matching CSS variable. For values that don't fit an existing token (e.g. `1.75rem`, `2.25rem`, `5rem`), **either** promote them to named tokens in `theme.css` (e.g. `--hero-sub-size`) **or** fold them into the closest canonical token. Do not leave any raw literals. 3. Remove every inline `style={{ '--font-size-code': '…' }}` override from the 5 code-block slides — the CodeBlock component already reads `var(--font-size-code)`, so the overrides are now no-ops. 4. Replace every hardcoded SVG `fontSize="10"|"11"|"13"|etc` in diagram components and slide SVGs with either a prop wired to a CSS variable via `getComputedStyle` **or** a plain var reference if the component is styled through CSS (not SVG attribute). For SVG `` elements, prefer `style={{ fontSize: 'var(--slide-text-svg-md)' }}` over the `fontSize="13"` attribute — SVG text respects CSS `font-size`. 5. **Do not change any visible size in Phase 6a.** Run `bun run build` + visual diff (screenshot every slide, compare against pre-change) to confirm zero visual regression. ### Phase 6b migration steps (aggressive bump) 1. In `src/styles/theme.css`, raise `--slide-text-h2` from `3.5rem` → `4.5rem`, `--slide-text-body` from `1.35rem` → `2rem`, `--slide-text-code` from `0.85rem` → `1.25rem`, `--slide-text-svg-sm/md/lg` proportionally. 2. Hero tokens (`--hero-*`) stay **unchanged**. The title slide is the one place where font sizes are calibrated against a pixel-perfect template replica; changing them breaks parity. This is explicit in Codex's risk note. 3. Proceed to Phase 7 overflow verification. ### Single-source-of-truth acceptance test The rule: **no raw font-size declarations outside `theme.css`.** Token definitions (`--slide-text-…: 2rem;`) live only in `src/styles/theme.css`; every other `font-size` consumer reads through `var(…)`. Concrete greps after Phase 6a (all must return **zero** lines): - `rg -n "font-size\\s*:" src/styles/slides.css src/index.css | rg -v "var\\("` — every font-size declaration outside `theme.css` must use `var(…)`. - `rg -n "--slide-text-|--hero-|--font-size-" src/styles/slides.css | rg -v "^\\S+:\\d+:\\s*(/\\*| \\*)"` — token *definitions* live only in `theme.css`; `slides.css` may *reference* them via `var(…)` but must not redeclare them. (If this hits false positives on comments, tighten the filter.) - `rg -n "fontSize" src/components src/slides | rg -v "var\\(" | rg -v "^\\S+:\\d+:\\s*//"` — every inline `fontSize` in TSX must go through a CSS variable. Together these mean editing a single token in `src/styles/theme.css` resizes the corresponding class everywhere it's used — which is what the user asked for: "I can change sizes on all slides from one place for further iteration." Files touched: - `src/styles/theme.css` (new tokens, aliases) - `src/styles/slides.css` (~40+ font-size declarations → vars) - `src/index.css` (base font-size stays 16px, no change) - 5 code-block slides (remove inline override) - All 7 diagram components (replace hardcoded SVG fontSize) - Any slide with inline SVG fontSize (enumerate via grep before starting) - `src/slides/TitleSlide.tsx` (no change — hero classes now read from `--hero-*` tokens) --- ## Phase 7 — Overflow verification + build gate Adding top chrome (~100 px vertical budget) + body size bump (2rem vs 1.35rem, ~48% larger) **will** push many slides past the viewport edge. Codex flags this as understated — accept. Assume at least half the body slides will need adjustment, not "some". Steps: 1. `bun install && bun run build` — must pass with zero TypeScript errors before any visual verification starts. Any type regression from the Phase 4/5/6 refactors gets fixed here. 2. `bun run dev` and open `http://localhost:5173/dou-days-2026/`. 3. For each slide 1–24, use the Chrome extension to navigate via `next`, take a screenshot at 1920×1080 (and a second at 1440×900 to check small-laptop responsive behaviour). 4. Check for text cropped by the bottom panel, top chrome, or the `.slide { overflow: hidden }` guard. 5. For any slide that overflows: **split into two slides** (register a new entry in `src/slides/index.ts` — the `slides` array lives there, not `src/App.tsx`) or trim copy. **Do not** introduce new font sizes to fix overflow — that violates the centralised-typography goal. 6. After all slides pass visual check, run `bun run build` a second time and confirm bundle size has not regressed beyond the size of the new sonar assets. Record overflow incidents in a scratch section at the bottom of this plan file while iterating (use as a working todo list, not a final deliverable). --- ## Phase 8 — Documentation sync By the time this phase runs, Phase 1 Step 3 has already moved the five files out of `docs/` and deleted the empty directory. Phase 8 is just the content rewrites that depend on Phase 3–6 outcomes. In-file updates: - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/full-spec.md` (formerly `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md`) — rewrite "Chrome" and "Layout patterns" sections to describe the new cohesive-chrome rule (global logo + Kyiv badge + randomised sonar on every slide except title). Drop stale "no chrome overlay on content slides" wording. - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/cohesive-chrome.md` (NEW) — write the Phase 3–5 rules: sonar-pool picker logic using the `bodySonarFor(slideIndex, slideId)` helper (slideId-hashed for stability under reorder, see Phase 2), badge placement tokens, BioSlide-as-body-slide rationale. - `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/translation-constraints.md` (formerly `docs/SLIDE_TRANSLATION.md`) — Phase 5 already trimmed the BioSlide-specific table. Nothing more to do here. - `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/calibration-methodology.md` (formerly `docs/TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION.md`) — unchanged in substance; confirm relative links work after the move. - `CLAUDE.md` — update the "Design System" Quick rules section: chrome now applies globally; font sizes live only in `theme.css`; randomised sonar picked per slide index deterministically. Replace `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` link with `.claude/skills/design-system/`. - Sanity gate: `rg "docs/(DESIGN_SYSTEM|TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION|SLIDE_TRANSLATION|slides-with-blurred-background|slides-with-side-images)"` returns **zero** lines repo-wide. Final `docs/` state: directory does not exist. All knowledge lives in `.claude/skills/{design-system,pixel-perfect-translation}/`. --- ## Critical files (full list, for reference during execution) **New:** - `.claude/skills/design-system/SKILL.md` - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/cohesive-chrome.md` - `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/SKILL.md` - `src/components/GlobalChrome.tsx` - `public/sonar/dou-sonar-01.png` … `-N.png` **Moved (via `git mv` from `docs/` into skill references folders — all five files):** - `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` → `.claude/skills/design-system/references/full-spec.md` - `docs/slides-with-blurred-background.md` → `.claude/skills/design-system/references/blurred-background-pattern.md` - `docs/slides-with-side-images.md` → `.claude/skills/design-system/references/side-images-pattern.md` - `docs/TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION.md` → `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/calibration-methodology.md` - `docs/SLIDE_TRANSLATION.md` → `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/translation-constraints.md` - `docs/` directory itself: deleted **Modified:** - `src/styles/theme.css` (central typography tokens — Phase 6a add, Phase 6b bump) - `src/styles/slides.css` (~40+ font-size rules → CSS vars; body-slide chrome classes; hero token swap; bio-hero deletion; `docs/TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION.md` comment refs updated at lines 739 + 928) - `src/components/Slide.tsx` (accept `chrome`, `slideIndex`, and `slideId` props; render appropriate chrome) - `src/components/SonarPattern.tsx` (body variant + pool picker using `bodySonarFor`; drop `'speaker'`) - `src/components/SlideChrome.tsx` (add Kyiv badge alongside existing logo) - `src/components/Presentation.tsx` (thread `slideIndex`/`chrome` into ``; no chrome rendered here) - `src/components/CodeBlock.tsx` (already uses `var(--font-size-code)` — verify no inline override remains) - `src/components/diagram/ProcessBox.tsx`, `ContainerBox.tsx`, `ConnectionLine.tsx`, `StageNode.tsx`, `AgentActor.tsx`, `HumanActor.tsx` (replace hardcoded SVG fontSize with var references) - `src/types/slides.ts` (add `chrome` field; drop `heroVariant`) - `src/slides/index.ts` (no content change — this is where the `slides` array lives; **not** `src/App.tsx`) - `src/slides/TitleSlide.tsx` (add `chrome: 'hero'`) - `src/slides/BioSlide.tsx` (rewrite as body slide; preserve 5-stage reveal map) - 5 slides with inline `--font-size-code` overrides (WarcraftFun, SkillsActivator, AutoApproveHook, AgentWorkflow, AgentTraces — MetaSkillsSlide has no override) - Any slide with inline SVG `fontSize=` (enumerate via `rg -n "fontSize" src/slides/` before starting Phase 6a) - `public/ASSET_PROVENANCE.md` (add rows for new sonar pool; extend licensing note) - `.claude/skills/design-system/references/full-spec.md` (formerly `docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` — rewrite chrome + layout sections) - `.claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/references/translation-constraints.md` (formerly `docs/SLIDE_TRANSLATION.md` — trim BioSlide table) - `CLAUDE.md` (update Design System section; re-point doc links to `.claude/skills/…` paths) **Deleted (if unreferenced after rework):** - `public/dou-rings-speaker.png` - `.bio-hero*` CSS block in `src/styles/slides.css` --- ## Reusable existing utilities (do not reinvent) - **`computeScale` in `src/components/Slide.tsx`** — already implements 1920×1080 fixed-stage scaling. Title slide keeps using it. - **`src/components/SlideElements.tsx`** — `SectionHeader`, `SlideItem`, `Code`, `Emphasis`, `Quote`, `SlideLink`. All slide rewrites (BioSlide in particular) must compose these, not raw HTML. - **`CodeBlock` in `src/components/CodeBlock.tsx`** — the canonical code-panel. The 6 slides migrating off inline `--font-size-code` stay on this component. - **`SonarPattern` srcRect+stretch technique** (existing `title` variant) — reuse the same wrapper+inner-img pattern for every new body variant so calibration stays consistent. - **`SlideChrome` logo wrapper** — extend in place (add the Kyiv badge alongside the existing logo ``), do not fork. - **Existing `@fontsource/ibm-plex-sans`** import in `src/index.css` — already covers 400/600/700 latin + cyrillic. No new font work needed. - **`vite-plugin-imagemin`** already compresses PNGs at build time — just drop new sonar assets into `public/sonar/`, no extra step. --- ## Verification End-to-end acceptance criteria: 1. **Skills activate correctly.** In a fresh Claude Code session in this repo, mention "I need to add a slide" → the `design-system` skill should surface as a relevant skill. Mention "reproduce template slide 4 pixel-perfect" → the `pixel-perfect-translation` skill should surface. 2. **Font-size single-source-of-truth check.** Open `src/styles/theme.css`, change `--slide-text-body` from `2rem` to `3rem`. Every body slide's paragraph text resizes. Nothing else requires editing. Revert. 3. **Chrome parity check.** For every slide except `title`, a screenshot at 1920×1080 shows: - A sonar backdrop (varies between slides deterministically — navigating back to slide 3 shows the same sonar every time) - DOU logo top-right (unchanged from current title slide) - "Київ, 2026" top-left - The bottom panel (Timer, TerminalInput, SlideProgress) unchanged 4. **Title slide still pixel-perfect.** Compare screenshot of slide 1 against the pre-change version — zero visual diff. 5. **No overflow.** `bun run dev`, navigate every slide, visually confirm no text cropped. 6. **Build passes.** `bun run build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors. Bundle size does not regress by more than the size of the new sonar assets. 7. **Grep cleanliness.** `rg "fontSize" src/slides` returns **zero** results. `rg "--font-size-code:" src/slides` returns **zero** results. `rg "#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}" src/slides` returns only SVG fill/stroke constants. 8. **Doc parity.** `.claude/skills/design-system/references/full-spec.md` reflects the new cohesive-chrome rule. `CLAUDE.md` Quick rules match implementation. `rg "docs/(DESIGN_SYSTEM|TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION|SLIDE_TRANSLATION|slides-with-blurred-background|slides-with-side-images)"` returns **zero** results repo-wide. The `docs/` directory no longer exists. 9. **Skill content completeness.** Every invariant that previously lived in `docs/` is now reachable from one of the two SKILL.md files (either inline or via a link into its `reference/` folder). No knowledge is lost in the migration. Use the Chrome extension (`http://localhost:5173/dou-days-2026/`) for every visual verification step as mandated by `CLAUDE.md`. --- ## Phase ordering & cross-phase dependencies ``` 1a. Skills scaffold (SKILL.md + references/ with shims pointing at existing docs/) 1b. Validate skills activate in a fresh session 1c. Atomic docs/ → references/ move (git mv all 5 files; update every docs/ reference repo-wide; delete docs/) 2. Extract sonar pool from template PPTX → public/sonar/ 3. Generalize SonarPattern (body variant + picker), SlideChrome (Kyiv badge), add GlobalChrome wrapper 4. Extend Slide.tsx with chrome mode; thread slideIndex + slideId from Presentation; add chrome field to SlideDefinition; TitleSlide sets chrome:'hero' 5. Rework BioSlide (drop hero; preserve reveal map); delete .bio-hero CSS, speaker sonar asset, provenance row, SonarPattern speaker variant atomically; trim translation-constraints.md 6a. Add typography tokens; convert every font-size call site to var(); visual output unchanged 6b. Bump token values to aggressive sizes; hero tokens untouched 7. bun install + bun run build (typecheck gate); browser verification at 1920×1080 and 1440×900; split/trim any overflowing slide via src/slides/index.ts 8. Content rewrites in moved reference files; CLAUDE.md update; final sanity grep ``` Phases 1 and 2 are independent and can run in parallel. Phase 3 depends on Phase 2 (needs assets to reference). Phase 4 depends on Phase 3. Phase 5 depends on Phase 4. Phase 6a can start as early as Phase 1 (it touches theme.css/slides.css/slides, none of which Phase 1 moves). Phase 6b must wait for Phase 6a. Phase 7 is the gate; Phase 8 is cleanup.

3	description: Generates images using Google Gemini 3 Pro API. Use when asked to create, generate, or design images for slides, presentations, or visual content.
4	allowed-tools: Bash(bun:*), Read, Write
5	---
6	
7	# Image Generation with Gemini 3 Pro
8	
9	Generate images for slides and presentations using Google's Gemini 3 Pro image generation API.
10	
11	## Prerequisites
12	
13	Ensure `.env.local` exists with your Gemini API key:
14	```
15	GEMINI_API_KEY=your-api-key-here
16	```
17	
18	## Usage
19	
20	Run the generation script:
21	```bash
22	bun --env-file=.env.local .claude/skills/generate-image/scripts/generate.ts --prompt "your image description" --ratio "16:9" --output "output.png"
23	```
24	
25	### Parameters
26	
27	| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
28	|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
29	| `--prompt` | Yes | - | Image description |
30	| `--ratio` | No | 16:9 | Aspect ratio: 16:9, 4:3, 1:1, 4:5 |
31	| `--output` | No | Auto-generated | Output filename |
32	
33	### Examples
34	
35	```bash
36	# Widescreen slide background
37	bun --env-file=.env.local .claude/skills/generate-image/scripts/generate.ts \
38	  --prompt "Abstract dark terminal with glowing code, cyberpunk aesthetic" \
39	  --ratio "16:9"
40	
41	# Square icon
42	bun --env-file=.env.local .claude/skills/generate-image/scripts/generate.ts \
43	  --prompt "Minimalist code icon, orange and green on dark background" \
44	  --ratio "1:1" \
45	  --output "code-icon.png"
46	```
47	
48	## Output
49	
50	**Images** are saved to `public/` with auto-generated timestamps if no filename specified.
51	
52	**Prompts** are automatically logged to `src/prompts/` as JSON files containing:
53	- `filename`: Output image filename
54	- `prompt`: The prompt used to generate the image
55	- `ratio`: Aspect ratio used
56	- `timestamp`: Generation timestamp (ISO format)
57	- `model`: Gemini model used
58	
59	This allows you to reference and regenerate images later using the same prompts.
60	
61	## Troubleshooting
62	
63	- **API key not found**: Ensure `.env.local` exists with `GEMINI_API_KEY`
64	- **Generation failed**: Check API key validity and quota
65	- **Model error**: The script uses `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` model
66

file.content:
---
name: generate-image
description: Generates images using Google Gemini 3 Pro API. Use when asked to create, generate, or design images for slides, presentations, or visual content.
allowed-tools: Bash(bun:*), Read, Write
---

# Image Generation with Gemini 3 Pro

Generate images for slides and presentations using Google's Gemini 3 Pro image generation API.

## Prerequisites

Ensure `.env.local` exists with your Gemini API key:
```
GEMINI_API_KEY=your-api-key-here
```

## Usage

Run the generation script:
```bash
bun --env-file=.env.local .claude/skills/generate-image/scripts/generate.ts --prompt "your image description" --ratio "16:9" --output "output.png"
```

### Parameters

| Parameter | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `--prompt` | Yes | - | Image description |
| `--ratio` | No | 16:9 | Aspect ratio: 16:9, 4:3, 1:1, 4:5 |
| `--output` | No | Auto-generated | Output filename |

### Examples

```bash
# Widescreen slide background
bun --env-file=.env.local .claude/skills/generate-image/scripts/generate.ts \
  --prompt "Abstract dark terminal with glowing code, cyberpunk aesthetic" \
  --ratio "16:9"

# Square icon
bun --env-file=.env.local .claude/skills/generate-image/scripts/generate.ts \
  --prompt "Minimalist code icon, orange and green on dark background" \
  --ratio "1:1" \
  --output "code-icon.png"
```

## Output

**Images** are saved to `public/` with auto-generated timestamps if no filename specified.

**Prompts** are automatically logged to `src/prompts/` as JSON files containing:
- `filename`: Output image filename
- `prompt`: The prompt used to generate the image
- `ratio`: Aspect ratio used
- `timestamp`: Generation timestamp (ISO format)
- `model`: Gemini model used

This allows you to reference and regenerate images later using the same prompts.

## Troubleshooting

- **API key not found**: Ensure `.env.local` exists with `GEMINI_API_KEY`
- **Generation failed**: Check API key validity and quota
- **Model error**: The script uses `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` model

---
name: design-system
description: Apply the DOU Days 2026 deck design system when editing slides, changing colors or typography, updating palette or font tokens, adjusting chrome (logo, Kyiv badge, sonar backdrop), or matching the DOU template look. Use whenever touching src/slides/, src/styles/, or any component that renders slide chrome.
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Edit
---

# DOU Days 2026 Design System

The design language for this deck: DOU magenta/mint/violet palette, IBM Plex Sans typography, fixed 1920×1080 stage, cohesive top chrome (logo + Kyiv badge) + randomised sonar backdrop on every slide except the title. Every visual rule lives here or in the referenced files.

## Invariants

- **Palette**: only `--dou-*` tokens (`--dou-magenta`, `--dou-mint`, `--dou-violet`, `--dou-deep-purple`, `--dou-cyan`). Legacy `--terminal-*` tokens alias these and are kept for backwards compatibility — do not introduce new rules that use them.
- **Typography**: IBM Plex Sans (self-hosted via `@fontsource/ibm-plex-sans`, 400/600/700, latin + cyrillic). JetBrains Mono only inside `<code>` / `CodeBlock`.
- **Font sizes live only in `src/styles/theme.css`.** Tokens: `--slide-text-h2`, `--slide-text-body`, `--slide-text-code`, `--slide-text-caption`, `--slide-text-svg-sm|md|lg` for body slides; `--hero-*` for the title slide. Every other file reads these via `var(...)`. Editing a token in `theme.css` resizes every usage in the deck.
- **2–3 font sizes per slide.** Body slides: heading (`--slide-text-h2`) + body (`--slide-text-body`). Code slides add `--slide-text-code`. Data-viz slides add SVG tokens. Title slide uses the `--hero-*` tokens. Never introduce a new size to fix one slide.
- **Chrome on every slide except `title`**: DOU logo top-right + "Київ, 2026" top-left (from `SlideChrome`) + randomised sonar backdrop (from `SonarPattern variant="body"`). The title slide uses its own calibrated chrome + sonar.

## Forbidden

- Inline `fontSize`/`style={{fontSize: …}}` in TSX (including SVG text). Always go through a CSS variable.
- Hardcoded hex colors in JSX `style={}`. Exception: SVG `fill`/`stroke` JS constants.
- New CSS classes without first checking `src/styles/slides.css` / `terminal.css` for an existing one.
- Glow, scanline, phosphor, or flicker effects. Legacy variables resolve to `none` / `transparent` on purpose.
- Changing the hero tokens (`--hero-*`) during a typography retune — title slide pixel-perfect calibration depends on them.

## Checklist before shipping any UI change

1. `rg "fontSize" src/slides src/components | rg -v "var\("` returns **zero** hits.
2. `rg "font-size\s*:" src/styles/slides.css src/index.css | rg -v "var\("` returns **zero** hits.
3. `rg "#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}" src/slides` returns only SVG fill/stroke constants.
4. `bun run build` passes with no TypeScript errors.
5. Visual parity verified via Chrome extension on `bun run dev` — screenshot at 1920×1080 and 1440×900, check no overflow against the bottom panel or top chrome.

## Adding or editing a slide

- Compose `SectionHeader`, `SlideItem`, `Code`, `Emphasis`, `Quote`, `SlideLink` from `src/components/SlideElements.tsx`. Do not use raw HTML headings or lists.
- Use `CodeBlock` from `src/components/CodeBlock.tsx` for code panels. Do not set `fontSize` on it — it already reads `var(--font-size-code)`.
- If content overflows, split into two slides in `src/slides/index.ts` or trim copy. Never introduce a new font size.
- Register new slides in `src/slides/index.ts` (the `slides` array). `src/App.tsx` just renders `<Presentation slides={slides} />`.

## Deep reference

- [references/full-spec.md](references/full-spec.md) — complete palette, typography, layout patterns, chrome rules, do/don'ts
- [references/blurred-background-pattern.md](references/blurred-background-pattern.md) — full-screen image + blurred content box pattern
- [references/side-images-pattern.md](references/side-images-pattern.md) — full-height side-image layout pattern
- [references/cohesive-chrome.md](references/cohesive-chrome.md) — body-slide chrome composition, sonar pool picker (`bodySonarFor`), badge placement tokens

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.

Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". - update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.

Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.

input.content: --- name: pixel-perfect-translation description: Recreate a DOU Days 2026 template slide in React/CSS at pixel parity. Use when building or tuning a hero slide, extracting assets from the template deck, matching template positioning exactly, calibrating a fixed 1920×1080 stage, translating hero copy into Ukrainian without breaking layout, or working with EMU, srcRect, or stretch image cropping. allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Edit, Bash(unzip:*), Bash(ffmpeg:*), Bash(magick:*), Bash(identify:*) --- # Pixel-perfect translation from the DOU Days 2026 template The title slide is a coordinate-for-coordinate replica of the DOU Days 2026 dark speaker template. This skill captures the methodology for keeping it that way and for adding any future template-matched slide. ## Fixed-stage model - The stage is always `1920×1080`. Every hero coordinate in `src/styles/slides.css` is an absolute stage pixel. - `src/components/Slide.tsx` (`computeScale` + ResizeObserver) scales the stage to fit the viewport via `transform: scale(min(w/1920, h/1080))`. Coordinates never change with viewport size — the transform does all the work. - EMU → px: PPTX uses English Metric Units. Divide any EMU value in the template XML by **4762.5** to get stage px (derived from `914400 EMU / 192 px-per-slide-width`). ## srcRect + stretch image replication PPTX shapes can crop+scale a source image via `` (percentage crop on each edge) + ``. Replicate in CSS without a pre-cropped PNG: 1. Outer wrapper `div` — final stage box (e.g. `width: 364px; height: 84px; position: absolute; top: 27px; left: 1501px;`). 2. Inner `` — original source scaled up so that the cropped region is exactly the wrapper size. If crop is `l=10.42% r=6.751% t=15.672% b=15.679%`, visible width is `100% - 10.42% - 6.751% = 82.829%` of source, so `inner-img-width = wrapper-width / 0.82829 ≈ 440px`. 3. Inner `` offset — shift the image left/up by `crop-origin × scale`, i.e. `left: -(0.1042 × 440)px ≈ -46px`, `top: -(0.15672 × 522)px ≈ -82px`. 4. `overflow: hidden` on the wrapper clips the overflow. Same technique used in `src/components/SonarPattern.tsx` and `src/components/SlideChrome.tsx`. ## Extraction workflow (adding a new sonar / ring asset from the template) ```bash # 1. Export DOU Days 2026 dark speaker template as PPTX # (https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/REDACTED) # 2. Unzip into a temp folder unzip template.pptx -d template-raw # 3. Inspect ppt/slides/slideN.xml for elements — note the rId (image ref) and # + (position + size in EMU) # 4. Find the image file ls template-raw/ppt/media/image*.png identify template-raw/ppt/media/imageN.png # source dimensions # 5. Copy into public/sonar/ with a descriptive name cp template-raw/ppt/media/imageN.png public/sonar/dou-sonar-N.png # 6. Document in public/ASSET_PROVENANCE.md: # - source slide, rId, srcRect (if applicable), target stage coords in px, role # - preserve the existing "DOU Days 2026 speaker rights" licensing note ``` ## Font-size uplift for large halls The template sizes read small in a 400+ seat hall. Bump hero font sizes by ~30–40% over the template value. Calibrated values live in `--hero-*` tokens in `src/styles/theme.css`: - `--hero-title-size: 94px` - `--hero-tagline-size: 56px` - `--hero-speaker-size: 38px` - `--hero-badge-size: 34px` **Do not change these tokens** during a typography retune of body slides — hero parity depends on them. ## Ukrainian translation tuning order Cyrillic glyphs are ~1–2% wider than Latin at the same font size. When copy overflows a calibrated container: 1. **Rephrase** shorter (first choice — no calibration risk) 2. **Tighten tracking** (`letter-spacing`) by `-0.01em` increments 3. **Drop the font size** by one step — but never below the calibrated minimum 4. **Widen the container** — but never past the calibrated maximum (documented per slide in `references/translation-constraints.md`) 5. **Accept wrap** as last resort ## Deep reference - [references/calibration-methodology.md](references/calibration-methodology.md) — full EMU math, worked example for title slide, complete srcRect+stretch recipe - [references/translation-constraints.md](references/translation-constraints.md) — hero slide translation rules, glyph-count checklist

toolUseResult.content: --- name: pixel-perfect-translation description: Recreate a DOU Days 2026 template slide in React/CSS at pixel parity. Use when building or tuning a hero slide, extracting assets from the template deck, matching template positioning exactly, calibrating a fixed 1920×1080 stage, translating hero copy into Ukrainian without breaking layout, or working with EMU, srcRect, or stretch image cropping. allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Edit, Bash(unzip:*), Bash(ffmpeg:*), Bash(magick:*), Bash(identify:*) --- # Pixel-perfect translation from the DOU Days 2026 template The title slide is a coordinate-for-coordinate replica of the DOU Days 2026 dark speaker template. This skill captures the methodology for keeping it that way and for adding any future template-matched slide. ## Fixed-stage model - The stage is always `1920×1080`. Every hero coordinate in `src/styles/slides.css` is an absolute stage pixel. - `src/components/Slide.tsx` (`computeScale` + ResizeObserver) scales the stage to fit the viewport via `transform: scale(min(w/1920, h/1080))`. Coordinates never change with viewport size — the transform does all the work. - EMU → px: PPTX uses English Metric Units. Divide any EMU value in the template XML by **4762.5** to get stage px (derived from `914400 EMU / 192 px-per-slide-width`). ## srcRect + stretch image replication PPTX shapes can crop+scale a source image via `` (percentage crop on each edge) + ``. Replicate in CSS without a pre-cropped PNG: 1. Outer wrapper `div` — final stage box (e.g. `width: 364px; height: 84px; position: absolute; top: 27px; left: 1501px;`). 2. Inner `` — original source scaled up so that the cropped region is exactly the wrapper size. If crop is `l=10.42% r=6.751% t=15.672% b=15.679%`, visible width is `100% - 10.42% - 6.751% = 82.829%` of source, so `inner-img-width = wrapper-width / 0.82829 ≈ 440px`. 3. Inner `` offset — shift the image left/up by `crop-origin × scale`, i.e. `left: -(0.1042 × 440)px ≈ -46px`, `top: -(0.15672 × 522)px ≈ -82px`. 4. `overflow: hidden` on the wrapper clips the overflow. Same technique used in `src/components/SonarPattern.tsx` and `src/components/SlideChrome.tsx`. ## Extraction workflow (adding a new sonar / ring asset from the template) ```bash # 1. Export DOU Days 2026 dark speaker template as PPTX # (https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/REDACTED) # 2. Unzip into a temp folder unzip template.pptx -d template-raw # 3. Inspect ppt/slides/slideN.xml for elements — note the rId (image ref) and # + (position + size in EMU) # 4. Find the image file ls template-raw/ppt/media/image*.png identify template-raw/ppt/media/imageN.png # source dimensions # 5. Copy into public/sonar/ with a descriptive name cp template-raw/ppt/media/imageN.png public/sonar/dou-sonar-N.png # 6. Document in public/ASSET_PROVENANCE.md: # - source slide, rId, srcRect (if applicable), target stage coords in px, role # - preserve the existing "DOU Days 2026 speaker rights" licensing note ``` ## Font-size uplift for large halls The template sizes read small in a 400+ seat hall. Bump hero font sizes by ~30–40% over the template value. Calibrated values live in `--hero-*` tokens in `src/styles/theme.css`: - `--hero-title-size: 94px` - `--hero-tagline-size: 56px` - `--hero-speaker-size: 38px` - `--hero-badge-size: 34px` **Do not change these tokens** during a typography retune of body slides — hero parity depends on them. ## Ukrainian translation tuning order Cyrillic glyphs are ~1–2% wider than Latin at the same font size. When copy overflows a calibrated container: 1. **Rephrase** shorter (first choice — no calibration risk) 2. **Tighten tracking** (`letter-spacing`) by `-0.01em` increments 3. **Drop the font size** by one step — but never below the calibrated minimum 4. **Widen the container** — but never past the calibrated maximum (documented per slide in `references/translation-constraints.md`) 5. **Accept wrap** as last resort ## Deep reference - [references/calibration-methodology.md](references/calibration-methodology.md) — full EMU math, worked example for title slide, complete srcRect+stretch recipe - [references/translation-constraints.md](references/translation-constraints.md) — hero slide translation rules, glyph-count checklist
