Use when you want to test any specific agent (AI Chat, Proofreader, Rewriter,
Humanizer, Paraphraser, AI Grader, etc.) or verify overall AI Editor health
against local CAPI.
- product-dev:bootstrap-coda: Run `make bootstrap` on the Coda repo non-interactively. One-time setup for a fresh Coda clone. Use when Coda is not bootstrapped, pm2 is not found, or make bootstrap needs to run.
- product-dev:local-development: This skill should be used when the user asks to "set up local coda", "run local capi", "configure local extension", "set up agent development", "stop all services", "stop local dev", "shutdown local", or mentions local development environment, dev.coda.io, make dev-go, make bootstrap, grammarly coda bridge, pm2 monit, or integration agents onboarding.
- product-dev:bootstrap-capi: One-time CAPI environment setup (hosts, certs, config). Run after setup-credentials and bootstrap-coda. Use when /etc/hosts is missing CAPI entries, Coda CA is not in the Java trust store, or CAPI fails to connect to Coda.
- product-dev:feedbacks-io: This skill should be used when the user asks to "check pattern stats", "search feedbacks patterns", "get ATLR", "pattern acceptance rate", or mentions feedbacks.grammarly.io, pattern performance, cheetah patterns, or G2 patterns. Queries Feedbacks pattern data via a bundled Python CLI.
- product-dev:setup-credentials: Configure all credentials needed for local development (Artifactory, GitLab, AWS SSO, NPM auth, Gradle). One-time setup for a fresh machine. Use when credentials are missing or expired.
- product-dev:extension-go-agents-local-testing: This skill should be used when the user asks to "test go agents", "test superhuman go", "open go sidebar", "test weather agent", "test go notch", "click the notch", "open the extension sidebar", or mentions go agents local testing, superhuman go notch, extension sidebar automation, chrome side panel testing, or agent sidebar interaction.
- review:code-tour: This skill should be used when walking through MR/PR code changes file by file with interactive stops. Presents one file at a time, waits for user input before proceeding.
- review:security-review: Dedicated security review step that delegates to the /security-review command. Run by the mango federated orchestrator before standard-review.
- review:project-guidelines: This skill should be used when reading project-specific review conventions from CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md. Extracts review criteria, validation matrices, and mandatory steps that customize the code review process.
- review:fetch-mr-context: This skill should be used when fetching MR/PR metadata from GitHub or GitLab. Supports two modes: 'branch' (search by branch name) and 'url' (fetch by parsed URL components). Shared by pre-review and review-url commands.
- review:vibes-context: This skill should be used when extracting context from vibes sessions linked in MR/PR descriptions. Downloads session transcripts and parses them for developer intent, key decisions, challenges, and incomplete work to inform code review focus areas.
- review:standard-review: This skill should be used when performing Claude's standard code review analysis. Reviews changed code for quality, performance, and architecture issues with confidence scoring. Generates structured JSON findings and deduplicates against existing comments.
- review:review-url: Analyze GitHub/GitLab merge requests and provide intelligent review guidance from URL. TRIGGER when: reviewing a PR, analyzing a merge request, pasting a GitHub PR URL, pasting a GitLab MR URL, code review from URL. Also trigger when: user shares a pull request link, asks to review changes at a URL.
- review:git-archaeology: Trace structural intent behind code organization to detect when PRs reverse deliberate decisions. TRIGGER when: investigating why code was structured a certain way, tracing design decisions, checking if a refactoring reverses a prior fix, analyzing structural changes in a PR. Also trigger when: diff contains function renames, step reordering, module merging or splitting.
- review:pre-screening: This skill should be used for fast eligibility checks before a full code review. Determines whether changes warrant review or can be skipped (closed/merged/draft MRs, auto-generated files only, trivial changes).
- review:respond-review: Systematically address code review comments on merge requests. TRIGGER when: user asks to "respond to review", "address review comments", "fix review feedback", "handle PR comments", "resolve review threads". Also trigger when: user wants to reply to or fix code review findings.
- review:code-walkthrough: This skill should be used when offering an educational walkthrough of MR/PR changes before formal code review. Presents a choice between quick summary, full guided tour, or skip. Delegates file-by-file tour to code-tour skill. Used by review-url command only.
- review:assess-complexity: This skill should be used when assessing the complexity of code changes to determine review routing. Classifies changes as 'mechanical', 'trivial', or 'complex' and identifies applicable review agents based on changed file patterns.
- review:consolidate-results: This skill should be used when consolidating findings from multiple review agents into a unified report. Handles collecting background agent results (checking auto-delivered notifications before falling back to TaskOutput), deduplication, confidence boosting, and severity categorization.
- review:evaluate-cicd: This skill should be used when evaluating CI/CD pipeline health during a code review. Assesses pipeline status, categorizes failures, and checks quality gates for MRs/PRs.
- review:design-patterns: Analyze code for GoF design patterns, anti-patterns, and improvement suggestions. TRIGGER when: analyzing class hierarchy, reviewing code structure, spotting god objects or shotgun surgery, identifying refactoring opportunities, or evaluating design abstractions. Also trigger when: code review surfaces structural concerns, or a PR touches factory/handler/strategy/observer abstractions.
- review:detect-agents: This skill should be used when detecting CLI agent availability for multi-agent code review. Checks if Codex and Gemini CLI tools are installed and usable, with nvm fallback for Node.js version requirements.
- review:fetch-existing-comments: This skill should be used when fetching existing review comments from a GitHub PR or GitLab MR for duplicate detection. Retrieves inline comments and transforms them to a unified format.
- review:git-context: This skill should be used when gathering git blame and history context for code review. Analyzes modification patterns, identifies hotspots, and provides confidence adjustments based on historical context.
- review:discover-ci-jobs: Discover CI/CD job definitions from workflow files in the repository checkout. TRIGGER when: code review needs to understand what CI jobs exist, what they check, and what tool versions they use. Also trigger when: deduplicating review findings against deterministic CI checks.
- review:gather-changes: This skill should be used when gathering git change statistics for code review. Collects commit count, file change stats, and changed file list between two git refs.
- review:launch-agents: This skill should be used when launching background review agents for deep multi-agent code review. Launches pr-review-toolkit agents and CLI agents (Codex, Gemini) in parallel based on the assessed complexity and applicable agent list.
- onboarding:user-survey: Conduct a multi-dimensional user experience interview. Detects key technologies from exploration results, pre-analyzes git history for skill signals, and asks targeted per-technology questions with analogy discovery.
- onboarding:write-metadata: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'write podcast metadata', 'create podcast.json', 'save episode statuses', or 'finalize season metadata'.
- onboarding:generate-transcript: Generate a podcast transcript via GPT-5.4 from Claude's draft narrative, validate dialogue line count, and retry if too short. Extracts previousSummary for episode chaining.
- onboarding:plan-season: Generate a 10-12 episode podcast season plan from codebase exploration results. Follows progressive structure from big picture to advanced topics.
- onboarding:synthesize-audio: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'synthesize episode audio', 'convert transcript to audio', 'generate podcast MP3', or 'run TTS synthesis'.
- onboarding:draft-narrative: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'draft episode narrative', 'write podcast outline', 'create narrative arc', or 'build episode structure' from deep exploration results. Generates a detailed narrative outline using podcast-craft.md best practices for conflict-driven structure and active learning techniques.
- onboarding:generate-clips: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'generate video clips', 'create podcast clips', 'make TikTok clips from episode', or is generating short video excerpts from a podcast episode transcript. Creates 2-3 vertical video clips (1080x1920) per episode using Remotion.
- onboarding:onboarding-podcast: This skill should be used when the user asks to 'generate onboarding podcast', 'create codebase podcast', 'podcast walkthrough', 'audio onboarding', or mentions generating podcast episodes for learning a codebase. Explores a codebase, plans a podcast season, generates transcripts via GPT, and synthesizes audio via OpenAI TTS.
- onboarding:explore-codebase: Launch parallel Explore agents to map a codebase for onboarding podcast generation. Collects structure, core logic, and supporting systems findings.
- onboarding:explore-episode-topic: Launch 2-3 Explore agents to deeply research a specific podcast episode topic. Each agent focuses on a different aspect: primary abstractions, connections/dependencies, and edge cases.
- onboarding:generate-website: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'generate podcast website', 'create podcast player site', 'build podcast web player', or is setting up a website to serve onboarding podcast transcripts and audio. Creates a React + Bun website with audio player, transcript sync, and episode thumbnails.
- auto-approval:debug-auto-approval: This skill should be used when the user asks to "debug auto-approval", "auto-approval not working", "check auto-approval", "fix auto-approval", "verify auto-approval hooks", or reports that commands are not being auto-approved. Provides complete diagnostics for the auto-approval plugin including hook status, logs, cache, LLM connectivity, and MCP tool catalog.
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codexcli:codexcli: Guides OpenAI Codex CLI installation, configuration, and usage on Grammarly infrastructure. TRIGGER when: codexcli, install codex cli, setup codex cli, codex brew install, codex cli login, codex model proxy, grammarly codex, codex cli troubleshooting. Also trigger when: deep codebase analysis, complex debugging, code generation, or code review via codex.
- codexcli:codex-plan-review: Review implementation plans iteratively using OpenAI Codex CLI as a second opinion, revising each round until approved (max N rounds, default 5). TRIGGER when reviewing an implementation plan, seeking external validation of a plan, validating a plan after plan mode exits, comparing Claude's plan against another model, or critiquing a freshly drafted plan before execution.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.subject: Phase 5: Rework BioSlide into body slide
content.description: Record BioSlide reveal map. Rewrite as two-column body slide using SlideElements. Delete .bio-hero CSS, speaker sonar asset + provenance row, SonarPattern speaker variant atomically. Trim translation-constraints.md.

content.subject: Phase 6a: Centralize typography tokens (mechanical)
content.description: Add new tokens to theme.css. Convert every font-size in slides.css, inline SVG fontSize in diagram components + slides, and --font-size-code overrides in 5 slides to var() references. Zero visual change. Verify via three grep acceptance tests.

content.subject: Phase 8: Documentation sync
content.description: Rewrite full-spec.md chrome/layout sections. Write cohesive-chrome.md (Phase 3-5 rules + bodySonarFor). Update CLAUDE.md Design System section. Final sanity grep for stale docs/ paths.

content.subject: Phase 1a: Scaffold skills with shim references to existing docs/
content.description: Create .claude/skills/design-system/ and .claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/ with SKILL.md files (concise frontmatter + body) and references/ subdirectories containing shim files that link to the existing docs/ paths. Skills are activatable; docs/ is untouched.

content.subject: Phase 1c: Atomic docs/ migration into references/
content.description: git mv all 5 docs/ files into the respective references/ directories. Update every docs/ path repo-wide (CLAUDE.md, slides.css:739, slides.css:928, cross-links inside the moved files). Replace Phase 1a shims with real content. Delete docs/ directory.

content.subject: Phase 2: Extract sonar asset pool from DOU template PPTX
content.description: Download DOU Days 2026 dark speaker template as PPTX, unzip, extract 6-8 sonar/ring backgrounds to public/sonar/. Record provenance in ASSET_PROVENANCE.md. User may need to perform PPTX export step.

content.subject: Phase 6b: Aggressive font size bump
content.description: Raise --slide-text-h2 to 4.5rem, --slide-text-body to 2rem, --slide-text-code to 1.25rem. Proportional bump for SVG tokens. Hero --hero-* tokens stay unchanged (title slide pixel-perfect).

content.subject: Phase 3: Generalize SonarPattern + SlideChrome + GlobalChrome
content.description: Extend SonarPattern with body variant + pool picker (bodySonarFor). Extend SlideChrome with Kyiv badge (single-mode, no variant prop). Add GlobalChrome wrapper composing both.

content.subject: Phase 4: Wire chrome into Slide.tsx
content.description: Extend Slide.tsx with chrome mode + slideIndex + slideId props. Thread from Presentation.tsx. Add chrome field to SlideDefinition. TitleSlide sets chrome:'hero'. Retire heroVariant.

content.subject: Phase 7: Build gate + overflow verification
content.description: bun install + bun run build (zero TS errors). bun run dev. Screenshot every slide at 1920x1080 and 1440x900 via Chrome extension. Split or trim overflowing slides via src/slides/index.ts. Second bun run build to confirm bundle size.

lastPrompt: I want to converge design system into together: (1) translate design system into project-level skill (2) translate pixel perfect translation process into project-level skill (3) title slide is the cus…

content.text: Both SKILL.md files are defined. Jumping straight to Phase 1c — the shim files would be replaced by `git mv` anyway, so I'll consolidate. First, remove shims and do the atomic migration.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

Use when building or tuning a hero slide, extracting assets from the template deck, matching template positioning exactly, calibrating a fixed 1920×1080 stage, translating hero copy into Ukrainian without breaking layout, or working with EMU, srcRect, or stretch image cropping.

Also trigger when: understanding unfamiliar services, investigating incidents, tracing cross-repo dependencies, building pre-refactor context.
- cicd:update-merge-request: Creates or updates a GitLab merge request with an intelligent title and description. TRIGGER when: create merge request, update merge request, open MR, submit MR, create MR, make MR, push changes for review, gitlab pull request. Also trigger when: branch has unpushed commits and user wants to share work for code review, or implementation is complete and needs team review.
- cicd:check-my-changelists: Show all open merge requests and pull requests with approval status and review comments. TRIGGER when: checking changelists, showing pending MRs, reviewing CL status, or listing open merge requests. Also trigger when: determining what needs review, identifying blockers, or planning merge order.
- cicd:install-sonarqube-cli: Installs the SonarSource sonar CLI and authenticates it against sonarqube.grammarly.io, auto-retrieving the user token via Chrome. TRIGGER when: installing sonarqube cli, sonar CLI setup, configuring sonar auth, sonar auth login needed, sonarqube token retrieval. Also trigger when: cicd:sonarqube is about to run and sonar is not on PATH, or command -v sonar fails, or sonar auth status reports not logged in.
- cicd:github: Execute GitHub operations using gh CLI for pull requests, repository exploration, and API calls. TRIGGER when: running gh commands, checking PR status, handling GitHub 404 errors, exploring external GitHub repos, making GitHub API requests. Also trigger when: git push/fetch/pull to github.com remotes, OAuth workflow scope errors, or GraphQL resolution failures.
- cicd:debug-renovate: This skill should be used when the user asks to "debug renovate", "why isn't renovate creating MRs", "renovate not working", "dependency updates missing", or mentions a stuck Renovate pipeline or missing merge requests. Provides a systematic diagnostic workflow covering local config, inherited presets, the Dependency Dashboard, pipeline logs, and schedule alignment.
- design-system:design-system-doc: This skill should be used when the user asks to "build a UI component", "implement a form", "add a button", "use design tokens", "find an icon", or mentions Origin, @superhuman/origin, React components, design system, or component library. Provides complete reference for the Origin design system — React components, CSS/JS design tokens, icons, typography, color, accessibility patterns, and content guidelines.
- gemini:gemini: Executes Google Gemini CLI for deep codebase analysis, complex debugging, and code generation. Use when tracing code paths, analyzing hard-to-reproduce bugs, or generating new implementations.
- golden-path:mcp-server: This skill should be used when the user asks to "build an MCP server", "create a FastMCP server", "expose an API as a Claude tool", or mentions FastMCP, model context protocol, or MCP server implementation. Creates production-ready MCP servers using FastMCP with 80-90% less boilerplate.
- golden-path:java: Switch JDK versions using SDKMAN. Use when builds fail due to Java version mismatch, or when you need to use a specific Java version for a project.
- golden-path:tracing-onboarding: This skill should be used when the user asks to "integrate tracing", "set up observability-tracing", "add kafka tracing", "update plane config", "verify spans", or mentions tracing onboarding for Spring Boot services.
- golden-path:java-http-client: This skill should be used when the user asks to "configure Spring HTTP client", "set up RestClient with retry", "add circuit breaker", "migrate legacy RestClient", or mentions grammarly.http.client, RestClientBuilder, WebClientBuilder, or Resilience4j. Provides complete configuration reference for Grammarly's HTTP client library with Spring Boot.
- golden-path:dx-product-events: This skill should be used when the user asks to "add event tracking", "integrate DX PlatformX", "track tool adoption", "collect usage metrics", or mentions DX product events, PlatformX, or developer tool analytics. Provides implementation patterns for integrating DX PlatformX event tracking into Go, Python, and JavaScript services.
- k8s:argocd-access: This skill should be used when the user asks to "access ArgoCD", "open ArgoCD", "get ArgoCD URL", "ArgoCD login", mentions ArgoCD endpoints, or needs help with "App not assigned" errors in ArgoCD. Helps users find the correct ArgoCD URL and Opal resource for any cluster type and environment at Grammarly.
- k8s:helm-add-mesh-route: This skill should be used when the user asks to "add a mesh route", "allow service-to-service traffic", "authorize linkerd access", or mentions adding network access via the service mesh on K8s. Configures linkerd ServerAuthorization in *-plane-config repositories.
- k8s:k8s-keda-unpause: Resume KEDA autoscaling by removing paused-scale-in annotation from a ScaledObject. Restores normal metric-based scaling.
- k8s:helm-add-rate-limit: This skill should be used when the user asks to "add rate limiting", "throttle service traffic", "set up a rate limit policy", or mentions limiting requests between services via linkerd on K8s. Configures linkerd RateLimitPolicy in *-plane-config repositories.
- k8s:k8s-okta-relogin: Clear OIDC cache and re-authenticate to a Kubernetes cluster via Okta. Use when kubectl commands fail with authentication errors or tokens have expired.
- k8s:k8s-keda-pause: Pause KEDA scale-in to prevent scaling down. Optionally scale up the workload.
- k8s:k8s-tools-install: Installs Kubernetes CLI tools on macOS including Homebrew, kubectl, and Grammarly RBAC access. Use when setting up k8s environment, configuring kubectl, or needing kubernetes-okta-access.
- meta:hook-dev: Create, configure, and debug Claude Code hooks — event-driven handlers in settings.json that execute automatically during the agent lifecycle. TRIGGER when: configuring PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop/SessionStart/PermissionRequest hooks, writing hook shell scripts, setting up auto-formatting or security gates, debugging hooks that don't fire or block incorrectly, understanding exit codes or matcher syntax. Also trigger when: user wants deterministic enforcement ("always format on save", "block dangerous commands"), or asks about hook handler types (command/http/prompt/agent).
- meta:skill-dev: Create, optimize, and validate Claude Code skills and subagents with research-backed patterns for maximum activation reliability. TRIGGER when: task involves writing a SKILL.md, crafting skill descriptions or frontmatter, structuring a skill directory, designing a slash command, packaging reusable instructions, codifying a workflow, extending Claude with domain expertise, creating a subagent definition, or writing an agent file for .claude/agents/
- meta:plugin-dev: Create, scaffold, and configure Claude Code plugins with proper directory layout, manifest configuration, and data storage conventions for the superhuman-aidev marketplace. TRIGGER when: user asks to "create a plugin", "scaffold a plugin", "understand plugin structure", "organize plugin components", "set up plugin.json", "configure plugin settings", "store plugin data", "XDG directories", or needs guidance on plugin architecture. Also trigger when: user needs help with plugin directory layout, manifest configuration, or data storage conventions. For creating specific components (skills, hooks), defer to meta:skill-dev or meta:hook-dev.
- misc:warcraft-work-sounds: This skill should be used when the user asks to "set up warcraft sounds", "play peon sounds", "work sounds stop hook", "zug zug sounds", "session sounds", "notification sounds", or mentions warcraft work audio, peon audio, stop hook sounds, lifecycle sounds, or playing sounds when Claude finishes or needs attention. Sets up Warcraft II peon/peasant sounds as Claude Code hooks across the session lifecycle: a "What?" sound when Claude needs permission (Notification), and a random ready/yes/complete sound when a session ends (Stop).
- misc:post-mango-release-notes: Generate and post release notes for @grammarly/mango code review agent to Slack. TRIGGER when: user asks to "generate mango release notes", "post mango release notes", "mango release", "code review agent release", or mentions #eng-ai-code-review release updates. Also trigger when: mango PRs have been merged and user wants to announce code review agent changes.
- misc:post-marketplace-release-notes: Generate and post release notes for the superhuman-aidev marketplace to Slack. TRIGGER when: user asks to "generate release notes", "post release notes", "release notes for merged PRs", "marketplace release", or mentions #eng-superhuman-aidev-plugins channel updates. Also trigger when: PRs have been merged and user wants to announce plugin changes.
- product-dev:test-ai-editor-agents: Guide for testing AI Editor agents locally using the Claude Chrome extension.
Covers the full workflow: connecting Chrome to a local Coda document, verifying
CAPI WebSocket connectivity, discovering all available agents in the sidebar,
and testing each agent interactively with pass/fail criteria.

Use when you want to test any specific agent (AI Chat, Proofreader, Rewriter,
Humanizer, Paraphraser, AI Grader, etc.) or verify overall AI Editor health
against local CAPI.
- product-dev:feedbacks-io: This skill should be used when the user asks to "check pattern stats", "search feedbacks patterns", "get ATLR", "pattern acceptance rate", or mentions feedbacks.grammarly.io, pattern performance, cheetah patterns, or G2 patterns. Queries Feedbacks pattern data via a bundled Python CLI.
- product-dev:bootstrap-coda: Run `make bootstrap` on the Coda repo non-interactively. One-time setup for a fresh Coda clone. Use when Coda is not bootstrapped, pm2 is not found, or make bootstrap needs to run.
- product-dev:setup-repositories: This skill should be used when the user asks to "clone repositories", "setup repos", "missing repos", "onboard repositories", "product dev repos", "where are my repos", or mentions cloning coda, common-api, chimera, coda-packs, or front-end-web-monorepo for local product development.
- product-dev:extension-go-agents-local-testing: This skill should be used when the user asks to "test go agents", "test superhuman go", "open go sidebar", "test weather agent", "test go notch", "click the notch", "open the extension sidebar", or mentions go agents local testing, superhuman go notch, extension sidebar automation, chrome side panel testing, or agent sidebar interaction.
- product-dev:local-development: This skill should be used when the user asks to "set up local coda", "run local capi", "configure local extension", "set up agent development", "stop all services", "stop local dev", "shutdown local", or mentions local development environment, dev.coda.io, make dev-go, make bootstrap, grammarly coda bridge, pm2 monit, or integration agents onboarding.
- product-dev:setup-credentials: Configure all credentials needed for local development (Artifactory, GitLab, AWS SSO, NPM auth, Gradle). One-time setup for a fresh machine. Use when credentials are missing or expired.
- product-dev:setup-dev-tools: Install all required system tools for local development on a fresh Mac. Use when Docker, Bazel, mkcert, glab, or git-lfs is not installed, or when setting up a new machine for the first time.
- product-dev:bootstrap-capi: One-time CAPI environment setup (hosts, certs, config). Run after setup-credentials and bootstrap-coda. Use when /etc/hosts is missing CAPI entries, Coda CA is not in the Java trust store, or CAPI fails to connect to Coda.
- review:git-archaeology: Trace structural intent behind code organization to detect when PRs reverse deliberate decisions. TRIGGER when: investigating why code was structured a certain way, tracing design decisions, checking if a refactoring reverses a prior fix, analyzing structural changes in a PR. Also trigger when: diff contains function renames, step reordering, module merging or splitting.
- review:review-url: Analyze GitHub/GitLab merge requests and provide intelligent review guidance from URL. TRIGGER when: reviewing a PR, analyzing a merge request, pasting a GitHub PR URL, pasting a GitLab MR URL, code review from URL. Also trigger when: user shares a pull request link, asks to review changes at a URL.
- review:fetch-mr-context: This skill should be used when fetching MR/PR metadata from GitHub or GitLab. Supports two modes: 'branch' (search by branch name) and 'url' (fetch by parsed URL components). Shared by pre-review and review-url commands.
- review:code-walkthrough: This skill should be used when offering an educational walkthrough of MR/PR changes before formal code review. Presents a choice between quick summary, full guided tour, or skip. Delegates file-by-file tour to code-tour skill. Used by review-url command only.
- review:discover-ci-jobs: Discover CI/CD job definitions from workflow files in the repository checkout. TRIGGER when: code review needs to understand what CI jobs exist, what they check, and what tool versions they use. Also trigger when: deduplicating review findings against deterministic CI checks.
- review:standard-review: This skill should be used when performing Claude's standard code review analysis. Reviews changed code for quality, performance, and architecture issues with confidence scoring. Generates structured JSON findings and deduplicates against existing comments.
- review:respond-review: Systematically address code review comments on merge requests. TRIGGER when: user asks to "respond to review", "address review comments", "fix review feedback", "handle PR comments", "resolve review threads". Also trigger when: user wants to reply to or fix code review findings.
- review:gather-changes: This skill should be used when gathering git change statistics for code review. Collects commit count, file change stats, and changed file list between two git refs.
- review:fetch-existing-comments: This skill should be used when fetching existing review comments from a GitHub PR or GitLab MR for duplicate detection. Retrieves inline comments and transforms them to a unified format.
- review:vibes-context: This skill should be used when extracting context from vibes sessions linked in MR/PR descriptions. Downloads session transcripts and parses them for developer intent, key decisions, challenges, and incomplete work to inform code review focus areas.
- review:pre-screening: This skill should be used for fast eligibility checks before a full code review. Determines whether changes warrant review or can be skipped (closed/merged/draft MRs, auto-generated files only, trivial changes).
- review:launch-agents: This skill should be used when launching background review agents for deep multi-agent code review. Launches pr-review-toolkit agents and CLI agents (Codex, Gemini) in parallel based on the assessed complexity and applicable agent list.
- review:detect-agents: This skill should be used when detecting CLI agent availability for multi-agent code review. Checks if Codex and Gemini CLI tools are installed and usable, with nvm fallback for Node.js version requirements.
- review:git-context: This skill should be used when gathering git blame and history context for code review. Analyzes modification patterns, identifies hotspots, and provides confidence adjustments based on historical context.
- review:code-tour: This skill should be used when walking through MR/PR code changes file by file with interactive stops. Presents one file at a time, waits for user input before proceeding.
- review:consolidate-results: This skill should be used when consolidating findings from multiple review agents into a unified report. Handles collecting background agent results (checking auto-delivered notifications before falling back to TaskOutput), deduplication, confidence boosting, and severity categorization.
- review:assess-complexity: This skill should be used when assessing the complexity of code changes to determine review routing. Classifies changes as 'mechanical', 'trivial', or 'complex' and identifies applicable review agents based on changed file patterns.
- review:design-patterns: Analyze code for GoF design patterns, anti-patterns, and improvement suggestions. TRIGGER when: analyzing class hierarchy, reviewing code structure, spotting god objects or shotgun surgery, identifying refactoring opportunities, or evaluating design abstractions. Also trigger when: code review surfaces structural concerns, or a PR touches factory/handler/strategy/observer abstractions.
- review:project-guidelines: This skill should be used when reading project-specific review conventions from CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md. Extracts review criteria, validation matrices, and mandatory steps that customize the code review process.
- review:security-review: Dedicated security review step that delegates to the /security-review command. Run by the mango federated orchestrator before standard-review.
- review:evaluate-cicd: This skill should be used when evaluating CI/CD pipeline health during a code review. Assesses pipeline status, categorizes failures, and checks quality gates for MRs/PRs.
- onboarding:onboarding-podcast: This skill should be used when the user asks to 'generate onboarding podcast', 'create codebase podcast', 'podcast walkthrough', 'audio onboarding', or mentions generating podcast episodes for learning a codebase. Explores a codebase, plans a podcast season, generates transcripts via GPT, and synthesizes audio via OpenAI TTS.
- onboarding:draft-narrative: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'draft episode narrative', 'write podcast outline', 'create narrative arc', or 'build episode structure' from deep exploration results. Generates a detailed narrative outline using podcast-craft.md best practices for conflict-driven structure and active learning techniques.
- onboarding:user-survey: Conduct a multi-dimensional user experience interview. Detects key technologies from exploration results, pre-analyzes git history for skill signals, and asks targeted per-technology questions with analogy discovery.
- onboarding:generate-transcript: Generate a podcast transcript via GPT-5.4 from Claude's draft narrative, validate dialogue line count, and retry if too short. Extracts previousSummary for episode chaining.
- onboarding:generate-clips: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'generate video clips', 'create podcast clips', 'make TikTok clips from episode', or is generating short video excerpts from a podcast episode transcript. Creates 2-3 vertical video clips (1080x1920) per episode using Remotion.
- onboarding:explore-codebase: Launch parallel Explore agents to map a codebase for onboarding podcast generation. Collects structure, core logic, and supporting systems findings.
- onboarding:write-metadata: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'write podcast metadata', 'create podcast.json', 'save episode statuses', or 'finalize season metadata'.
- onboarding:explore-episode-topic: Launch 2-3 Explore agents to deeply research a specific podcast episode topic. Each agent focuses on a different aspect: primary abstractions, connections/dependencies, and edge cases.
- onboarding:synthesize-audio: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'synthesize episode audio', 'convert transcript to audio', 'generate podcast MP3', or 'run TTS synthesis'.
- onboarding:plan-season: Generate a 10-12 episode podcast season plan from codebase exploration results. Follows progressive structure from big picture to advanced topics.
- onboarding:generate-website: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'generate podcast website', 'create podcast player site', 'build podcast web player', or is setting up a website to serve onboarding podcast transcripts and audio. Creates a React + Bun website with audio player, transcript sync, and episode thumbnails.
- auto-approval:debug-auto-approval: This skill should be used when the user asks to "debug auto-approval", "auto-approval not working", "check auto-approval", "fix auto-approval", "verify auto-approval hooks", or reports that commands are not being auto-approved. Provides complete diagnostics for the auto-approval plugin including hook status, logs, cache, LLM connectivity, and MCP tool catalog.
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codexcli:codex-plan-review: Review implementation plans iteratively using OpenAI Codex CLI as a second opinion, revising each round until approved (max N rounds, default 5). TRIGGER when reviewing an implementation plan, seeking external validation of a plan, validating a plan after plan mode exits, comparing Claude's plan against another model, or critiquing a freshly drafted plan before execution.
- codexcli:codexcli: Guides OpenAI Codex CLI installation, configuration, and usage on Grammarly infrastructure. TRIGGER when: codexcli, install codex cli, setup codex cli, codex brew install, codex cli login, codex model proxy, grammarly codex, codex cli troubleshooting. Also trigger when: deep codebase analysis, complex debugging, code generation, or code review via codex.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- pixel-perfect-translation: Recreate a DOU Days 2026 template slide in React/CSS at pixel parity. Use when building or tuning a hero slide, extracting assets from the template deck, matching template positioning exactly, calibrating a fixed 1920×1080 stage, translating hero copy into Ukrainian without breaking layout, or working with EMU, srcRect, or stretch image cropping.
- generate-image: Generates images using Google Gemini 3 Pro API. Use when asked to create, generate, or design images for slides, presentations, or visual content.
- design-system: Apply the DOU Days 2026 deck design system when editing slides, changing colors or typography, updating palette or font tokens, adjusting chrome (logo, Kyiv badge, sonar backdrop), or matching the DOU template look. Use whenever touching src/slides/, src/styles/, or any component that renders slide chrome.

- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md: Update CLAUDE.md with learnings from this session

- gemini:init-gemini: Initialize GEMINI.md files as symlinks to CLAUDE.md files in repository
- golden-path:tracing-integrate-redis: Integrate Redis tracing using the golden-path tracing router skill.

- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver: Audit and improve CLAUDE.md files in repositories. Use when user asks to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md files. Also use when the user mentions "CLAUDE.md maintenance" or "project memory optimization".
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- aidev:restore-context: Restore working context for the current branch by analyzing commits, diffs, and MR/PR metadata. TRIGGER when: resuming work on a branch, starting a new session on an existing branch, needing to understand what was done previously, or picking up someone else's work. Also trigger when: context was lost after /clear, switching branches, or returning to a task after time away.
- aidev:session-upload: Upload session files to vibes server with secret scanning and metadata. Used internally by share-session and update-pull-request/update-merge-request. TRIGGER when session file paths are known and need uploading. Also trigger when caller provides UPLOAD_FILES context variable.
- aidev:teach-me: This skill should be used when the user asks to "teach me", "guided implementation", "learn by doing", "step by step quest", "TDD quest", or wants Claude to guide them through implementing a feature themselves instead of doing it for them.

- review:discover-ci-jobs: Discover CI/CD job definitions from workflow files in the repository checkout. - review:code-tour: This skill should be used when walking through MR/PR code changes file by file with interactive stops. Presents one file at a time, waits for user input before proceeding.
- review:review-url: Analyze GitHub/GitLab merge requests and provide intelligent review guidance from URL. - review:evaluate-cicd: This skill should be used when evaluating CI/CD pipeline health during a code review. Assesses pipeline status, categorizes failures, and checks quality gates for MRs/PRs.

- onboarding:generate-website: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'generate podcast website', 'create podcast player site', 'build podcast web player', or is setting up a website to serve onboarding podcast transcripts and audio. - onboarding:write-metadata: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'write podcast metadata', 'create podcast.json', 'save episode statuses', or 'finalize season metadata'.
- onboarding:plan-season: Generate a 10-12 episode podcast season plan from codebase exploration results. Follows progressive structure from big picture to advanced topics.
- onboarding:synthesize-audio: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'synthesize episode audio', 'convert transcript to audio', 'generate podcast MP3', or 'run TTS synthesis'.
- onboarding:draft-narrative: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'draft episode narrative', 'write podcast outline', 'create narrative arc', or 'build episode structure' from deep exploration results. - onboarding:explore-codebase: Launch parallel Explore agents to map a codebase for onboarding podcast generation. Collects structure, core logic, and supporting systems findings.

- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin

- codexcli:codex-plan-review: Review implementation plans iteratively using OpenAI Codex CLI as a second opinion, revising each round until approved (max N rounds, default 5). - update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

- onboarding:onboarding-podcast: This skill should be used when the user asks to 'generate onboarding podcast', 'create codebase podcast', 'podcast walkthrough', 'audio onboarding', or mentions generating podcast episodes for learning a codebase. - onboarding:explore-episode-topic: Launch 2-3 Explore agents to deeply research a specific podcast episode topic. Each agent focuses on a different aspect: primary abstractions, connections/dependencies, and edge cases.
- onboarding:generate-website: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'generate podcast website', 'create podcast player site', 'build podcast web player', or is setting up a website to serve onboarding podcast transcripts and audio. Creates a React + Bun website with audio player, transcript sync, and episode thumbnails.
- auto-approval:debug-auto-approval: This skill should be used when the user asks to "debug auto-approval", "auto-approval not working", "check auto-approval", "fix auto-approval", "verify auto-approval hooks", or reports that commands are not being auto-approved. Provides complete diagnostics for the auto-approval plugin including hook status, logs, cache, LLM connectivity, and MCP tool catalog.
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codexcli:codex-plan-review: Review implementation plans iteratively using OpenAI Codex CLI as a second opinion, revising each round until approved (max N rounds, default 5). TRIGGER when reviewing an implementation plan, seeking external validation of a plan, validating a plan after plan mode exits, comparing Claude's plan against another model, or critiquing a freshly drafted plan before execution.
- codexcli:codexcli: Guides OpenAI Codex CLI installation, configuration, and usage on Grammarly infrastructure. TRIGGER when: codexcli, install codex cli, setup codex cli, codex brew install, codex cli login, codex model proxy, grammarly codex, codex cli troubleshooting. Also trigger when: deep codebase analysis, complex debugging, code generation, or code review via codex.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# CLAUDE.md

## Project Overview

A custom presentation site for the Ukrainian-language talk "Невигадані історії AI-first трансформації в інженерних командах (про які неможливо мовчати)". Built with React and TypeScript, it uses the original Claude-Code-inspired terminal layouts (`$ pattern --foo` headings, `> ` bullets, command-based navigation) skinned in the DOU Days 2026 visual style — IBM Plex Sans font and the DOU magenta / mint / violet palette. Hosted on GitHub Pages at https://yermilov.github.io/dou-days-2026

See [docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md](docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md) for the design spec (colours, typography, layout patterns).

## Tech Stack

- **Runtime**: Bun
- **Framework**: React 18 + TypeScript
- **Bundler**: Vite
- **Syntax Highlighting**: react-syntax-highlighter
- **Hosting**: GitHub Pages (auto-deployed via GitHub Actions)

223	- **Font**: IBM Plex Sans (self-hosted, weights 400/600/700, latin + cyrillic). JetBrains Mono stays only inside `CodeBlock` / inline `<code>`.

## Design System

Full spec: **[docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md](docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md)** — read this before touching UI.

Quick rules:
- **Layout**: original Claude-Code-inspired terminal layouts. H2s follow the `$ pattern --foo` idiom; bullets use `> ` prefix; `TerminalInput` shows a `>` prompt; `SlideProgress` shows "context left until auto-compact N%".
- **Font**: IBM Plex Sans (self-hosted, weights 400/600/700, latin + cyrillic). JetBrains Mono stays only inside `CodeBlock` / inline `<code>`.
- **Palette**: use `--dou-*` tokens (`--dou-magenta`, `--dou-mint`, `--dou-violet`, `--dou-deep-purple`, …). Legacy `--terminal-*` tokens still exist but alias DOU values — don't introduce new rules that use them.
- **Background**: `--dou-bg-gradient` on the presentation container. No sonar pattern, no persistent chrome overlay.
- **2-sizes-per-slide rule** still applies: `--font-size-h2` heading + `--slide-text-normal` body. Title slide may add `--font-size-hero`.
- **No glow, scanline, phosphor, or flicker.** The legacy variables exist but resolve to `none` / `transparent`.
- **No hardcoded hex in slide JSX `style={}`.** Exception: SVG `fill` / `stroke` JS constants.

# Design System Hybrid aesthetic: **original Claude-Code-inspired layouts** (terminal command prompts, `$ pattern --foo` headings, `> ` bullets, `> ` input prompt, `context left until auto-compact` counter) rendered in the **DOU Days 2026 visual style** — IBM Plex Sans font and the DOU magenta / mint / violet colour palette. Content slides (2–27) have **no chrome overlay** and **no sonar background** — they keep the terminal layout (`$ pattern --foo` heading, `> ` bullets) rendered in DOU colours. The **title slide is a pixel-perfect reproduction of the DOU template title** — full-bleed sonar rings, `DOU|))` logo top-right, `Київ, 2026` badge, dark title rectangle with uppercase white text, and a magenta description block. It opts into this layout by setting `hero: true` on its `SlideDefinition`, which makes `Slide.tsx` wrap the content in a fixed 1920×1080 stage scaled uniformly via `transform: scale()` so absolute pixel positions stay identical at any window size. The bottom timer + terminal input bar remain unchanged, since they render outside the slide stage. See [TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION.md](./TITLE_SLIDE_CALIBRATION.md) for the methodology used to extract exact coordinates from the template PPTX and replicate them in CSS (including srcRect+stretch image transforms). ## Source templates The DOU palette, typography, and visual language in this doc are derived from the official DOU Days 2026 Google Slides templates provided by the conference: - Dark version (used here): - Light version: --- ## 1. Colours Defined in `src/styles/theme.css`. Always reference via CSS custom property in HTML/CSS. SVG `fill` / `stroke` attributes can use the hex directly. ### Structure | Token | Hex | Role | | --- | --- | --- | | `--dou-white` | `#FFFFFF` | Primary text | | `--dou-deep-purple` | `#270950` | Slide gradient, top-left | | `--dou-near-black` | `#0D021B` | Slide gradient, bottom-right | | `--dou-bg-solid` | `#0F0820` | CodeBlock / TerminalInput panel | | `--dou-bg-gradient` | `linear-gradient(135deg, #270950 0%, #0D021B 100%)` | Slide background | ### Accents | Token | Hex | Role | | --- | --- | --- | | `--dou-magenta` | `#FF16B1` | Primary accent — h1, `--flag` args, bullet prefix, input prompt | | `--dou-mint` | `#02FEB9` | Secondary accent — h2, `pattern` keyword, links | | `--dou-violet` | `#7626FF` | Tertiary accent — h3, alt headings | | `--dou-violet-light` | `#8D88FA` | Purple-ish token in syntax highlighting | | `--dou-cyan` | `#02D6FE` | Inline code, technical callouts | ### Tonal utilities | Token | Value | Role | | --- | --- | --- | | `--dou-white-dim` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.78)` | Secondary body text | | `--dou-white-muted` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.56)` | Tertiary / meta text | ### Legacy aliases The original `--terminal-*` tokens still exist in `theme.css`; they are **remapped to DOU values** so existing slide CSS and utility classes continue to render in the new palette: - `--terminal-orange` → magenta (`#FF16B1`) - `--terminal-green` → mint (`#02FEB9`) - `--terminal-blue` → violet (`#7626FF`) - `--terminal-cyan` → cyan (`#02D6FE`) - `--terminal-purple` → violet-light (`#8D88FA`) - `--terminal-bg*` → DOU surfaces - `--terminal-white*` → DOU whites - `--glow-*`, `--glow-text-*`, `--scanline-opacity`, `--noise-opacity`, `--flicker-intensity` → all zeroed / `none` Existing rules like `color: var(--terminal-orange)` render magenta automatically. Do **not** introduce new rules that reach for `--terminal-*` — use `--dou-*` directly. --- ## 2. Typography - **Single family: IBM Plex Sans** (self-hosted via `@fontsource/ibm-plex-sans`, weights 400 / 600 / 700 in both `latin` and `cyrillic` subsets; `font-display: swap`). - **Exception:** `CodeBlock` and inline `` keep **JetBrains Mono**. - Token: `--font-sans: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif;` ### 2-sizes-per-slide rule (unchanged from the original site) Every non-title slide uses exactly two sizes: - **Heading**: `--font-size-h2` (3.5rem) — the slide's `

` - **Text**: `--slide-text-normal` (1.35rem) — bullets, section headers, labels, everything else Title slide gets three sizes (hero + tagline + subtitle). --- ## 3. Layouts (unchanged from pre-rebrand) All original layout patterns from `CLAUDE.md` are preserved: 1. **Hero Title** (TitleSlide, FinalSlide) 2. **Header-Only** (SoftwareFactorySlide, AutoApproveHookSlide) 3. **Bullet List + Reveals** — `

` with `$ pattern --foo` structure + `SlideItem` components with `revealStage >= N` guards 4. **Two-Column: Text + Code** 5. **Two-Column: Text + Visual** 6. **Full Image** (`.image-slide`) 7. **Data Visualisation** (custom SVG) 8. **Timeline** — git-log layout Every slide h2 follows the `$ pattern --command` idiom: ```tsx

${' '} pattern{' '} --explore-and-have-fun

``` With the DOU palette aliases, this renders as dim-white `$` + mint `pattern` + magenta `--explore-and-have-fun` in IBM Plex Sans. --- ## 4. Chrome (interactive UI) - **TerminalInput**: `>` prompt in magenta IBM Plex Sans, dark `#0F0820` background, magenta focus border (no glow). - **SlideProgress**: `context left until auto-compact N%` copy, magenta progress-bar fill. - **Timer**: IBM Plex Sans tabular-nums, dim-white text. No persistent overlay on content slides. On the **title slide only**, the `SlideChrome` component renders the `DOU|))` logo top-right, and the `SonarPattern` component renders the full-bleed sonar rings (`public/dou-sonar-hero.png`). The `Київ, 2026` badge lives inside the title block (`.title-hero__tag`) rather than as free-floating chrome. --- ## 5. Do / Don't - ✅ IBM Plex Sans everywhere. CodeBlock keeps JetBrains Mono. - ✅ Use `--dou-*` tokens in new CSS. SVG may use hex constants in JS. - ✅ Keep the `$ pattern --foo` h2 idiom on content slides. - ✅ Keep the `> ` bullet / input prompt / section-header `// ` prefix. - ❌ No text-shadow / glow / phosphor / scanline effects. - ❌ No persistent chrome on content slide body (title slide is the only exception). - ❌ No hero sonar background on content slides (title slide is the only exception). - ❌ No hardcoded hex in slide JSX `style={}`. Exception: SVG attributes. - ❌ Don't exceed the 2-sizes-per-slide rule. Reduce content or split slides if they overflow. --- ## 6. Asset registry | File | Role | | --- | --- | | `public/dou-logo.png` | Browser tab favicon + title-slide top-right logo | | `public/dou-sonar-hero.png` | Title-slide full-bleed sonar background (extracted from the dark template) | | `public/favicon.png` | (unused; may be removed) | | `public/*.png|jpg|jpeg|mp4|wav` | Per-slide media | See `public/ASSET_PROVENANCE.md` for extraction details.

Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Also trigger when: diff contains function renames, step reordering, module merging or splitting.
- review:detect-agents: This skill should be used when detecting CLI agent availability for multi-agent code review. Checks if Codex and Gemini CLI tools are installed and usable, with nvm fallback for Node.js version requirements.
- review:respond-review: Systematically address code review comments on merge requests. TRIGGER when: user asks to "respond to review", "address review comments", "fix review feedback", "handle PR comments", "resolve review threads". Also trigger when: user wants to reply to or fix code review findings.
- review:evaluate-cicd: This skill should be used when evaluating CI/CD pipeline health during a code review. Assesses pipeline status, categorizes failures, and checks quality gates for MRs/PRs.
- review:code-tour: This skill should be used when walking through MR/PR code changes file by file with interactive stops. Presents one file at a time, waits for user input before proceeding.
- review:fetch-mr-context: This skill should be used when fetching MR/PR metadata from GitHub or GitLab. Supports two modes: 'branch' (search by branch name) and 'url' (fetch by parsed URL components). Shared by pre-review and review-url commands.
- review:project-guidelines: This skill should be used when reading project-specific review conventions from CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md. Extracts review criteria, validation matrices, and mandatory steps that customize the code review process.
- review:vibes-context: This skill should be used when extracting context from vibes sessions linked in MR/PR descriptions. Downloads session transcripts and parses them for developer intent, key decisions, challenges, and incomplete work to inform code review focus areas.
- onboarding:onboarding-podcast: This skill should be used when the user asks to 'generate onboarding podcast', 'create codebase podcast', 'podcast walkthrough', 'audio onboarding', or mentions generating podcast episodes for learning a codebase. Explores a codebase, plans a podcast season, generates transcripts via GPT, and synthesizes audio via OpenAI TTS.
- onboarding:plan-season: Generate a 10-12 episode podcast season plan from codebase exploration results. Follows progressive structure from big picture to advanced topics.
- onboarding:explore-codebase: Launch parallel Explore agents to map a codebase for onboarding podcast generation. Collects structure, core logic, and supporting systems findings.
- onboarding:synthesize-audio: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'synthesize episode audio', 'convert transcript to audio', 'generate podcast MP3', or 'run TTS synthesis'.
- onboarding:generate-transcript: Generate a podcast transcript via GPT-5.4 from Claude's draft narrative, validate dialogue line count, and retry if too short. Extracts previousSummary for episode chaining.
- onboarding:draft-narrative: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'draft episode narrative', 'write podcast outline', 'create narrative arc', or 'build episode structure' from deep exploration results. Generates a detailed narrative outline using podcast-craft.md best practices for conflict-driven structure and active learning techniques.
- onboarding:write-metadata: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'write podcast metadata', 'create podcast.json', 'save episode statuses', or 'finalize season metadata'.
- onboarding:explore-episode-topic: Launch 2-3 Explore agents to deeply research a specific podcast episode topic. Each agent focuses on a different aspect: primary abstractions, connections/dependencies, and edge cases.
- onboarding:generate-website: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'generate podcast website', 'create podcast player site', 'build podcast web player', or is setting up a website to serve onboarding podcast transcripts and audio. Creates a React + Bun website with audio player, transcript sync, and episode thumbnails.
- onboarding:user-survey: Conduct a multi-dimensional user experience interview. Detects key technologies from exploration results, pre-analyzes git history for skill signals, and asks targeted per-technology questions with analogy discovery.
- onboarding:generate-clips: This skill should be used when the agent needs to 'generate video clips', 'create podcast clips', 'make TikTok clips from episode', or is generating short video excerpts from a podcast episode transcript. Creates 2-3 vertical video clips (1080x1920) per episode using Remotion.
- auto-approval:debug-auto-approval: This skill should be used when the user asks to "debug auto-approval", "auto-approval not working", "check auto-approval", "fix auto-approval", "verify auto-approval hooks", or reports that commands are not being auto-approved. Provides complete diagnostics for the auto-approval plugin including hook status, logs, cache, LLM connectivity, and MCP tool catalog.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codexcli:codexcli: Guides OpenAI Codex CLI installation, configuration, and usage on Grammarly infrastructure. TRIGGER when: codexcli, install codex cli, setup codex cli, codex brew install, codex cli login, codex model proxy, grammarly codex, codex cli troubleshooting. Also trigger when: deep codebase analysis, complex debugging, code generation, or code review via codex.
- codexcli:codex-plan-review: Review implementation plans iteratively using OpenAI Codex CLI as a second opinion, revising each round until approved (max N rounds, default 5). TRIGGER when reviewing an implementation plan, seeking external validation of a plan, validating a plan after plan mode exits, comparing Claude's plan against another model, or critiquing a freshly drafted plan before execution.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Phase 1 is committed (`bb80031`). The two skills are activatable, `docs/` is gone, and all cross-references are updated.

**Status so far:**
- ✅ Phase 1a+1c: Skills scaffolded, 5 docs moved into `.claude/skills/*/references/`, stale refs rewritten
- ⏸️ Phase 2: Needs DOU template PPTX — the Google Slides deck at `docs.google.com/presentation/d/REDACTED`
- 🔜 Phases 3–8: blocked on or unblocked by Phase 2

Phase 2 (sonar pool extraction) needs the template PPTX exported from Google Slides. I want to check in before going further because the remaining work is substantial (new sonar assets, chrome refactor, BioSlide rewrite, ~40 font-size call sites, overflow verification across 24 slides).

questions.question: How should we source the additional sonar assets for Phase 2?

options.label: You'll export PPTX manually
options.description: You download the DOU deck from Google Slides as PPTX, drop it in the repo root (or tell me the path), and I extract sonar images + record provenance.

options.label: Try programmatic export
options.description: I hit https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/{id}/export/pptx and unzip. Only works if the deck is publicly readable; if it's private this will fail and fall back to option 1.

options.label: Defer Phase 2
options.description: Skip sonar extraction for now. Continue with Phase 6a (typography centralization) which is independent. Come back to sonar extraction later.

questions.question: How should I proceed with the rest of the work?

options.label: Keep going, commit per phase
options.description: Continue autonomously through all remaining phases. Commit after each phase so you can review as we go.

options.label: One big commit at the end
options.description: Do all phases, then one merge commit. Faster but harder to review.

options.label: Stop after the next phase
options.description: Let you review what's done and direct the next step.

input.command:
for img in /tmp/dou-template/raw/ppt/media/image*.png; do
  name=$(basename "$img")
  # Extract PNG dimensions from the IHDR chunk (bytes 16-23 after signature)
  dims=$(python3 -c "
import struct, sys
with open('$img', 'rb') as f:
    f.read(16)  # skip PNG signature + IHDR length + type
    w, h = struct.unpack('>II', f.read(8))
    print(f'{w}x{h}')
" 2>/dev/null)
  bytes=$(stat -f%z "$img")
  echo "$name $dims ${bytes}B"
done | sort -k2 | head -40
input.description: Extract PNG dimensions via Python

content.subject: Phase 6a: Centralize typography tokens (mechanical)
content.description: Add new tokens to theme.css. Convert every font-size in slides.css, inline SVG fontSize in diagram components + slides, and --font-size-code overrides in 5 slides to var() references. Zero visual change. Verify via three grep acceptance tests.

content.subject: Phase 8: Documentation sync
content.description: Rewrite full-spec.md chrome/layout sections. Write cohesive-chrome.md (Phase 3-5 rules + bodySonarFor). Update CLAUDE.md Design System section. Final sanity grep for stale docs/ paths.

content.subject: Phase 1a: Scaffold skills with shim references to existing docs/
content.description: Create .claude/skills/design-system/ and .claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/ with SKILL.md files (concise frontmatter + body) and references/ subdirectories containing shim files that link to the existing docs/ paths. Skills are activatable; docs/ is untouched.

content.subject: Phase 1c: Atomic docs/ migration into references/
content.description: git mv all 5 docs/ files into the respective references/ directories. Update every docs/ path repo-wide (CLAUDE.md, slides.css:739, slides.css:928, cross-links inside the moved files). Replace Phase 1a shims with real content. Delete docs/ directory.

content.subject: Phase 2: Extract sonar asset pool from DOU template PPTX
content.description: Download DOU Days 2026 dark speaker template as PPTX, unzip, extract 6-8 sonar/ring backgrounds to public/sonar/. Record provenance in ASSET_PROVENANCE.md. User may need to perform PPTX export step.

content.subject: Phase 6b: Aggressive font size bump
content.description: Raise --slide-text-h2 to 4.5rem, --slide-text-body to 2rem, --slide-text-code to 1.25rem. Proportional bump for SVG tokens. Hero --hero-* tokens stay unchanged (title slide pixel-perfect).

content.subject: Phase 3: Generalize SonarPattern + SlideChrome + GlobalChrome
content.description: Extend SonarPattern with body variant + pool picker (bodySonarFor). Extend SlideChrome with Kyiv badge (single-mode, no variant prop). Add GlobalChrome wrapper composing both.

content.subject: Phase 4: Wire chrome into Slide.tsx
content.description: Extend Slide.tsx with chrome mode + slideIndex + slideId props. Thread from Presentation.tsx. Add chrome field to SlideDefinition. TitleSlide sets chrome:'hero'. Retire heroVariant.

content.subject: Phase 7: Build gate + overflow verification
content.description: bun install + bun run build (zero TS errors). bun run dev. Screenshot every slide at 1920x1080 and 1440x900 via Chrome extension. Split or trim overflowing slides via src/slides/index.ts. Second bun run build to confirm bundle size.

input.command:
cd /tmp/dou-template/raw/ppt/slides && for slide in slide*.xml; do
  # Find all image references used in this slide  
  rels_file="_rels/${slide}.rels"
  imgs=$(grep -oE 'rId[0-9]+' "$slide" 2>/dev/null | sort -u | while read rid; do
    grep -oE "image[0-9]+\.(png|jpg|jpeg)" "$rels_file" 2>/dev/null | head -1
  done | sort -u | tr '\n' ' ')
  echo "$slide: $imgs"
done 2>&1 | head -30

file.content:
# Asset Provenance

## DOU Days 2026 template logo

| File | Source | Role |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `dou-logo.png` | DOU Days 2026 dark speaker template — slide 1, top-right embedded image | Browser tab favicon + title-slide top-right logo |
| `dou-sonar-hero.png` | DOU Days 2026 dark speaker template — full-bleed title-slide background | Title-slide sonar background |
| `dou-rings-speaker.png` | DOU Days 2026 dark speaker template — slide 6 background (`ppt/media/image19.png`) | Bio/speaker-slide rings background |

### Source

- Template: **DOU Days 2026 — Dark speaker template** (Google Slides)
- URL: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/REDACTED
- Extracted: 2026-04-18
- Dimensions at capture: `dou-logo.png` 226×63 px (source file 2048×571); `dou-sonar-hero.png` 1080×1080 px

### Extraction method

`dou-logo.png` + `dou-sonar-hero.png` were embedded in the template as `<image>`
elements referencing an in-memory `blob:` URL. Captured by running a small
in-page script (via Chrome DevTools) that calls `fetch()` on the blob URL,
converts the response to a base64 data URL, and triggers a local PNG download.

`dou-rings-speaker.png` was extracted from `ppt/media/image19.png` inside the
downloaded PPTX. The template renders it with `rot="5400000"` (90° CW) plus a
srcRect crop (`l=15.542% r=50.005% t=19.222% b=19.068%`). To keep the runtime
CSS simple, the asset was pre-rotated (90° CCW, equivalent visually since rings
are rotationally symmetric) and pre-cropped via:

```bash
ffmpeg -i image19.png -vf "transpose=2,crop=1263:694:394:1007" dou-rings-speaker.png
```

Output is 1263×694. In CSS it is applied at 50% opacity (matching the template's
`alphaModFix amt="50000"`).

### Usage rights

This asset belongs to the DOU Days 2026 conference. It is bundled here for the
speaker (`yaroslav.yermilov@grammarly.com`) to use as the browser-tab favicon
on the web presentation — the same purpose as the official templates the
conference distributed to speakers.

If this repo is ever forked / redistributed for purposes beyond the original
conference talk, confirm with DOU organisers that redistribution is
permitted, or remove this file and replace it with a self-authored equivalent.

CSS simple, the asset was pre-rotated (90° CCW, equivalent visually since rings
are rotationally symmetric) and pre-cropped via:

```bash
ffmpeg -i image19.png -vf "transpose=2,crop=1263:694:394:1007" dou-rings-speaker.png
```

Output is 1263×694. In CSS it is applied at 50% opacity (matching the template's
`alphaModFix amt="50000"`).

### Usage rights

This asset belongs to the DOU Days 2026 conference. It is bundled here for the
speaker (`yaroslav.yermilov@grammarly.com`) to use as the browser-tab favicon
on the web presentation — the same purpose as the official templates the
conference distributed to speakers.

If this repo is ever forked / redistributed for purposes beyond the original
conference talk, confirm with DOU organisers that redistribution is
permitted, or remove this file and replace it with a self-authored equivalent.

content.description: Record BioSlide reveal map. Rewrite as two-column body slide using SlideElements. Delete .bio-hero CSS, speaker sonar asset + provenance row, SonarPattern speaker variant atomically. Trim translation-constraints.md.

content.subject: Phase 6a: Centralize typography tokens (mechanical)
content.description: Add new tokens to theme.css. Convert every font-size in slides.css, inline SVG fontSize in diagram components + slides, and --font-size-code overrides in 5 slides to var() references. Zero visual change. Verify via three grep acceptance tests.

content.subject: Phase 8: Documentation sync
content.description: Rewrite full-spec.md chrome/layout sections. Write cohesive-chrome.md (Phase 3-5 rules + bodySonarFor). Update CLAUDE.md Design System section. Final sanity grep for stale docs/ paths.

content.subject: Phase 1a: Scaffold skills with shim references to existing docs/
content.description: Create .claude/skills/design-system/ and .claude/skills/pixel-perfect-translation/ with SKILL.md files (concise frontmatter + body) and references/ subdirectories containing shim files that link to the existing docs/ paths. Skills are activatable; docs/ is untouched.

content.subject: Phase 1c: Atomic docs/ migration into references/
content.description: git mv all 5 docs/ files into the respective references/ directories. Update every docs/ path repo-wide (CLAUDE.md, slides.css:739, slides.css:928, cross-links inside the moved files). Replace Phase 1a shims with real content. Delete docs/ directory.

content.subject: Phase 2: Extract sonar asset pool from DOU template PPTX
content.description: Download DOU Days 2026 dark speaker template as PPTX, unzip, extract 6-8 sonar/ring backgrounds to public/sonar/. Record provenance in ASSET_PROVENANCE.md. User may need to perform PPTX export step.

content.subject: Phase 6b: Aggressive font size bump
content.description: Raise --slide-text-h2 to 4.5rem, --slide-text-body to 2rem, --slide-text-code to 1.25rem. Proportional bump for SVG tokens. Hero --hero-* tokens stay unchanged (title slide pixel-perfect).

content.subject: Phase 3: Generalize SonarPattern + SlideChrome + GlobalChrome
content.description: Extend SonarPattern with body variant + pool picker (bodySonarFor). Extend SlideChrome with Kyiv badge (single-mode, no variant prop). Add GlobalChrome wrapper composing both.

content.subject: Phase 4: Wire chrome into Slide.tsx
content.description: Extend Slide.tsx with chrome mode + slideIndex + slideId props. Thread from Presentation.tsx. Add chrome field to SlideDefinition. TitleSlide sets chrome:'hero'. Retire heroVariant.

content.subject: Phase 7: Build gate + overflow verification
content.description: bun install + bun run build (zero TS errors). bun run dev. Screenshot every slide at 1920x1080 and 1440x900 via Chrome extension. Split or trim overflowing slides via src/slides/index.ts. Second bun run build to confirm bundle size.

content.text: Phase 3 + 4 done. Running a quick type-check before committing.

git add -A && git status --short && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Put every slide in the 1920x1080 stage with cohesive chrome

Phases 3 + 4 of the design-system convergence.

SonarPattern: add 'body' variant backed by the 3-image pool in
public/sonar/. Deterministic picker bodySonarFor(slideIndex, slideId)
hashes slideId so reordering slides only reshuffles the moved one.

SlideChrome: add top-left "Kyiv, 2026" badge at stage (55, 27), 34px /
84px high to match the logo band visually. Title slide opts out via
showCityBadge={false} because it has its own calibrated tag at (368,390)
inside the title block.

Slide: replace hero/heroVariant with a chrome mode ('hero' | 'global' |
'none'). 'hero' and 'global' both wrap content in the scaled stage; the
only difference is the sonar asset and whether the top-left badge renders.
slideIndex and slideId are threaded end-to-end so the sonar picker is
wired correctly.

Presentation: threads slideIndex + slideId + chrome into Slide with a
backwards-compat default (activeSlide.chrome ?? (activeSlide.hero ?
'hero' : 'global')). Bottom panel (Timer, TerminalInput, SlideProgress)
is untouched.

New CSS:
- .slide-container--staged removes container padding for staged slides
- .slide--body is the stage-sized analog of .slide--hero for body slides
- .sonar-pattern--body renders the body pool full-bleed at 35% opacity
- .slide-chrome__city-badge positions the Kyiv badge at stage (55, 27)

Build passes with no TS errors. Body slides now consistently show
randomised template sonar + logo + Kyiv badge inside the scaled stage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
